example_sentence: Sustained economic ___ breeds the conditions in which political extremism takes root.

thai_meaning: ความโกรธแค้นอย่างรุนแรง, สิ่งที่น่าชิงชัง
eng_definition: an extremely strong reaction of anger or moral indignation; an action that offends deeply
example_sentence: The publication of the leaked documents triggered widespread public ___ at the scale of the deception.

thai_meaning: เปิดเผย, ชัดเจน
eng_definition: done openly; not hidden or secret
example_sentence: The shift from ___ military intervention to covert influence operations confused international observers.

thai_meaning: ชัดเจนมาก, รู้สึกได้
eng_definition: able to be touched or felt; so intense as to seem almost tangible; obvious
example_sentence: The tension in the negotiating chamber was ___ long before any formal discussion had begun.

thai_meaning: อยู่ในสภาวะอันตราย, น่าวิตก
eng_definition: full of danger or uncertainty; perilous
example_sentence: The charity's finances were in a ___ state, with less than one month of reserves remaining.

thai_meaning: ผิดปกติทางจิตใจ, เป็นโรค
eng_definition: involving or caused by a mental or physical disorder; compulsive and obsessive
example_sentence: The institution exhibited a ___ aversion to any form of external scrutiny.

thai_meaning: เกี่ยวกับการลงโทษ, ทางอาญา
eng_definition: of or relating to the punishment of offenders under the legal system
example_sentence: The ___ system must balance deterrence against the need for genuine rehabilitation.

thai_meaning: ยาวนาน, เกิดขึ้นซ้ำทุกปี
eng_definition: lasting or existing for a long or apparently infinite time; occurring repeatedly
example_sentence: Underfunding of public infrastructure is a ___ problem that successive governments have failed to address.

thai_meaning: ผ่อนปรน, ยินยอมมากเกินไป

example_sentence: Critics argued that the ___ regulatory environment had enabled widespread financial misconduct.

thai_meaning: ทำให้คงอยู่ต่อไป, สืบต่อ
eng_definition: to make something continue indefinitely; to cause something to last or be preserved

thai_meaning: ชันมาก, รีบเร่งโดยไม่คิด
eng_definition: dangerously high or steep; done suddenly and without careful consideration
example_sentence: A ___ withdrawal of troops without a sustainable peace agreement could reignite the conflict.

thai_meaning: ข้ออ้าง, เหตุผลที่แกล้งแสร้ง
eng_definition: a reason given in justification that is not the true or main reason
example_sentence: The security concern appeared to be little more than a ___ for suppressing political dissent.

thai_meaning: ผัดวันประกันพรุ่ง, เลื่อนงาน
eng_definition: to delay or postpone action; to put off doing something until a later time
example_sentence: Governments tend to ___ on difficult structural reforms, preferring symbolic gestures instead.

thai_meaning: เผยแพร่, ทำให้แพร่กระจาย
eng_definition: to spread an idea, belief, or practice widely; to cause something to increase or multiply
example_sentence: Digital platforms ___ both accurate information and dangerous misinformation with equal speed.

thai_meaning: ชั่วคราว, ระหว่างรอการยืนยัน
eng_definition: arranged for the time being; temporary pending confirmation or revision
example_sentence: A ___ agreement was reached to allow humanitarian access while negotiations continued.

thai_meaning: ความรอบคอบ, วิจารณญาณที่ดี
eng_definition: the quality of being prudent; careful management and forward-looking judgement
example_sentence: Fiscal ___ demands that governments maintain reserves to respond to unforeseen economic shocks.

thai_meaning: เหตุผลพื้นฐาน, หลักเหตุผล
eng_definition: a set of reasons or a logical basis for a course of action or belief
example_sentence: The minister was asked to explain the ___ behind a decision that had alarmed the scientific community.

thai_meaning: ถอยหลัง, เดินถอยหลัง
eng_definition: directed or moving backwards; reverting to an earlier and worse condition
example_sentence: Repealing the equality legislation was condemned by rights groups as a ___ step.

thai_meaning: ความรังเกียจอย่างสุดขีด, ความขยะแขยง
eng_definition: a sense of deep disgust and loathing; a sudden strong reaction of aversion
example_sentence: The leaked images provoked widespread ___ and led to immediate calls for criminal prosecution.

thai_meaning: เข้มงวด, ละเอียดถี่ถ้วน
eng_definition: extremely thorough and careful; scrupulously accurate
example_sentence: Only ___ peer review can protect the scientific literature from methodological error.

thai_meaning: ศักดิ์สิทธิ์มากจนแตะต้องไม่ได้, ไม่ควรถูกวิจารณ์
eng_definition: regarded as too important to be questioned or criticised; inviolable
example_sentence: No institution, however powerful, should be regarded as ___ in a healthy democracy.

thai_meaning: การเสียดสี, การล้อเลียนเพื่อวิจารณ์
eng_definition: the use of humour, irony, or exaggeration to criticise or expose folly or vice
example_sentence: ___ has historically been one of the most powerful tools available to the politically powerless.

thai_meaning: ทางโลก, ไม่เกี่ยวกับศาสนา
eng_definition: not connected with religious matters; belonging to the worldly sphere
example_sentence: The constitution enshrines a strict ___ separation between state authority and religious institutions.

thai_meaning: มีอิทธิพลสูง, เป็นต้นแบบสำคัญ
eng_definition: strongly influencing later developments; highly original and important
example_sentence: The ___ work of the 1970s established the conceptual framework that still guides the field today.

thai_meaning: ฟังดูน่าเชื่อแต่ผิดพลาด, เป็นเพียงภาพลวงตา
eng_definition: superficially plausible but actually wrong; misleadingly attractive in appearance
example_sentence: The report contained ___ arguments that sounded compelling but collapsed under close scrutiny.

thai_meaning: ซื่อสัตย์มั่นคง, แน่วแน่
eng_definition: very loyal and committed; standing firm in support
example_sentence: Even the prime minister's most ___ supporters expressed concern about the direction of policy.

thai_meaning: เข้มงวดมาก, กวดขัน
eng_definition: strict, precise, and exacting; requiring exact standards to be met
example_sentence: The ___ eligibility criteria were criticised for excluding many who genuinely needed support.

thai_meaning: ครอบงำ, กดขี่
eng_definition: to bring under domination or control, especially by conquest; to make subservient
example_sentence: Colonial powers used economic dependency to ___ populations long after formal independence.

thai_meaning: เปราะบาง, ง่ายต่อการได้รับผลกระทบ
eng_definition: likely to be influenced or harmed by a particular thing; easily affected
example_sentence: Communities with poor sanitation are particularly ___ to waterborne disease outbreaks.

thai_meaning: อ่อนแอ, ไม่มีเหตุผลเพียงพอ
eng_definition: very weak or slight; lacking a strong basis; too thin to be substantial
example_sentence: The link between the policy and its claimed benefits was, at best, ___.

thai_meaning: วุ่นวาย, ขับเคี่ยว
eng_definition: loud, excited, and emotionally intense; full of commotion and upheaval
example_sentence: The ___ years following independence tested every institution the new state had established.

thai_meaning: ไร้มโนธรรม, เกินขอบเขตที่ยอมรับได้
eng_definition: not right or reasonable; going beyond what is acceptable; shockingly unfair
example_sentence: Exploiting vulnerable workers to maximise profit was described by the court as ___.

thai_meaning: ลังเล, แกว่งไปมาระหว่างสองสิ่ง
eng_definition: to waver between different opinions or actions; to be indecisive
example_sentence: A leader who ___ in the face of a crisis rapidly loses the confidence of those they lead.

thai_meaning: จุดเปลี่ยนสำคัญ, ช่วงเวลาชี้ขาด
eng_definition: an event or period marking a turning point in a course of action or state of affairs
example_sentence: The ruling was widely regarded as a ___ moment for environmental law in the country.

thai_meaning: สิ่งที่ผิดปกติจากแบบแผน, ความเบี่ยงเบน
eng_definition: a departure from what is normal or expected; an unusual occurrence
example_sentence: The isolated incident was dismissed as an ___ rather than evidence of a systemic pattern.

thai_meaning: ต่ำต้อยที่สุด, น่าสงสาร
eng_definition: experienced to the most severe or absolute degree; completely without dignity or hope
example_sentence: The report exposed levels of ___ poverty in wealthy nations that the public had chosen not to see.

thai_meaning: การละเว้น, การงดเว้นตนเอง
eng_definition: the practice of restraining oneself from indulging in something, typically alcohol or sex
example_sentence: The public health campaign promoted ___ from tobacco as the single most effective personal intervention.

thai_meaning: เหวลึก, ความลึกที่ไม่มีก้น
eng_definition: a deep or seemingly bottomless chasm; an immeasurably profound difference or distance
example_sentence: Without intervention, the country risked sliding into the ___ of civil conflict.

thai_meaning: สุภาษิต, คำพังเพยโบราณ
eng_definition: a short saying that expresses a general truth; a traditional proverb
example_sentence: The ___ that power corrupts finds ample illustration throughout political history.

thai_meaning: สิ่งที่เพิ่มเติม, รองหรือเสริม
eng_definition: a thing added to something else as a supplementary element
example_sentence: Counselling is offered as an ___ to the primary medical treatment, not a replacement for it.

thai_meaning: เกิดขึ้นโดยบังเอิญ, มาจากภายนอก
eng_definition: coming from an external source; arising from an unexpected or unplanned source
example_sentence: The discovery of oil was largely ___, transforming a small nation's economic prospects overnight.

thai_meaning: การยืนยัน, การรับรอง
eng_definition: the act of declaring something positively; emotional support or encouragement
example_sentence: The ruling was seen as a powerful ___ of the constitutional right to peaceful protest.

thai_meaning: พาดพิงถึง, บอกใบ้
eng_definition: to suggest or call attention to indirectly; to make a passing reference
example_sentence: The chairman carefully ___ to the financial irregularities without making any direct accusations.

thai_meaning: ความเป็นปฏิปักษ์, การต่อต้าน
eng_definition: active hostility or opposition between incompatible forces or ideas
example_sentence: Historical ___ between the two communities could not simply be dissolved by a signed agreement.

thai_meaning: อ้างสิทธิ์เกินหน้าที่, ฉวยโอกาส
eng_definition: to claim or seize something without justification; to take upon oneself without authority
example_sentence: The executive was accused of attempting to ___ legislative powers that belonged to parliament.

thai_meaning: หลีกเลี่ยง, ป้องกัน
eng_definition: to turn away from something; to prevent something undesirable from occurring
example_sentence: Decisive early action helped ___ a humanitarian catastrophe that would otherwise have been inevitable.

thai_meaning: ประกาศอย่างเปิดเผย, ยืนยันโดยตรง
eng_definition: to assert or declare openly and without shame
example_sentence: The candidate ___ her commitment to healthcare reform at every public appearance.

thai_meaning: ดูถูก, ทำให้ดูเล็กน้อย
eng_definition: to make someone or something seem unimportant or less valuable
example_sentence: A leader who ___ the contributions of others ultimately destroys the team's collective will.

thai_meaning: เริ่มพูดถึงหัวข้อที่ละเอียดอ่อน
eng_definition: to raise a sensitive or difficult subject for discussion
example_sentence: Neither side wanted to ___ the most contentious issue until a degree of trust had been built.

thai_meaning: จังหวะ, ลีลาการพูด
eng_definition: a modulation or inflection of the voice; the rhythmic flow of a sequence of sounds or words
example_sentence: The measured ___ of her speech conveyed authority without conveying urgency.

thai_meaning: ความสอดคล้องกัน, ความเป็นเนื้อเดียว
eng_definition: the quality of being logical, consistent, and forming a unified whole
example_sentence: The lack of policy ___ across departments had undermined the government's reform programme.

thai_meaning: การสมคบคิด, การร่วมมือลับๆ
eng_definition: secret or illegal cooperation to deceive others; fraudulent conspiracy
example_sentence: The investigation uncovered evidence of ___ between competitors to suppress consumer choice.

thai_meaning: ทำให้แย่ลงอีก, ซ้ำเติม
eng_definition: to make a problem or difficulty worse by adding to it
example_sentence: The policy failure was ___ by a communication strategy that appeared to minimise its severity.

thai_meaning: บรรลุจุดสูงสุด, สิ้นสุดด้วย
eng_definition: to reach the highest or final point of development; to result in
example_sentence: Years of negotiation finally ___ in a landmark agreement signed by all parties.

thai_meaning: ทำให้สูญเสียความเชื่อ, ทำให้หมดมนต์ขลัง
eng_definition: to make someone lose faith or idealism; to free from illusion
example_sentence: A decade of unfulfilled promises has served to ___ even the most committed supporters.

thai_meaning: ซ่อนความรู้สึกแท้จริง, แกล้งทำ
eng_definition: to conceal one's true motives, feelings, or beliefs; to act deceptively
example_sentence: A skilled political operator who could ___ effortlessly was a dangerous opponent.

thai_meaning: จางหายอย่างรวดเร็ว, ชั่วครั้งชั่วคราว
eng_definition: quickly fading from sight, memory, or existence; transitory
example_sentence: The ___ nature of political goodwill makes its conversion into lasting reform extremely difficult.

thai_meaning: พูดหรือทำโดยไม่ได้เตรียมล่วงหน้า
eng_definition: spoken or done without preparation; impromptu
example_sentence: Her ___ remarks at the press conference revealed far more about government thinking than the prepared statement.

thai_meaning: แง่มุม, ด้านหนึ่งของสิ่งที่ซับซ้อน
eng_definition: one side of something many-sided; a particular aspect of a subject
example_sentence: Every ___ of the proposed reform generated fierce debate among stakeholders.

thai_meaning: ความล้มเหลวย่อยยับ, ความอับอาย
eng_definition: a thing that is a complete failure, especially in a ludicrous or humiliating way
example_sentence: The product launch ___ cost the company both market share and investor confidence.

thai_meaning: กำลังจะเกิด, พร้อมเปิดเผย
eng_definition: ready or made available when required; willing to divulge information; about to happen
example_sentence: Officials were not ___ with the details that journalists and the public had a right to know.

thai_meaning: ชายขอบ, กลุ่มคนส่วนน้อยสุดขั้ว
eng_definition: the outer edge of an area or group; not part of the mainstream
example_sentence: Ideas once dismissed as ___ views have entered mainstream political discourse with alarming speed.

thai_meaning: บุกเบิก, ริเริ่มในสิ่งที่ไม่เคยมีมาก่อน
eng_definition: innovative and pioneering; introducing new methods or ideas that have never been tried
example_sentence: The ___ legislation transformed the rights of workers in ways that seemed impossible a generation earlier.

thai_meaning: ศักดิ์สิทธิ์, ได้รับความเคารพอย่างสูง
eng_definition: regarded as holy; greatly revered and honoured over time
example_sentence: The principle of judicial independence is one of the most ___ in any democratic constitutional order.

thai_meaning: ควบคุมและใช้ประโยชน์จาก
eng_definition: to control and use a natural resource or power effectively
example_sentence: The challenge of our era is to ___ technology in ways that serve all of humanity.

thai_meaning: สงบสุขสวยงาม, เหมือนในฝัน
eng_definition: like an idyll; extremely happy, peaceful, or picturesque
example_sentence: The ___ image of rural self-sufficiency often obscures the harsh realities of agricultural poverty.

thai_meaning: จุ่มตัว, ดำดิ่ง
eng_definition: to involve oneself deeply in an activity or interest; to submerge in a liquid
example_sentence: Researchers who fully ___ themselves in a community gain insights unavailable through surveys alone.

thai_meaning: ไม่สามารถรับเป็นหลักฐานได้, ไม่ยอมรับ
eng_definition: not allowed or accepted in a legal proceeding; not to be permitted
example_sentence: The judge ruled the confession ___ on the grounds that it had been obtained under duress.

thai_meaning: ไม่เลือกหน้า, ไม่มีการเลือกสรร
eng_definition: done without careful judgement; not making distinctions between different groups
example_sentence: ___ application of austerity measures fell disproportionately on those least able to bear the burden.

thai_meaning: ไม่สามารถยอมแพ้ได้, ไม่อาจพิชิตได้
eng_definition: impossible to subdue or defeat; stubbornly persistent
example_sentence: Her ___ spirit in the face of successive setbacks became an inspiration to her entire generation.

thai_meaning: เลวร้ายอย่างสุดขีด, ของนรก
eng_definition: relating to hell; used informally to express extreme frustration or exasperation
example_sentence: The ___ complexity of the tax code was cited as a barrier to economic participation.

thai_meaning: ไม่มีเกียรติ, น่าอับอาย
eng_definition: causing shame or loss of honour; not deserving glory
example_sentence: The administration's ___ handling of the crisis became a defining political liability.

thai_meaning: ไม่สามารถสนองได้, หิวโหยอยู่เสมอ
eng_definition: impossible to satisfy; having an insistent desire that cannot be fulfilled
example_sentence: Capital markets have an ___ appetite for short-term returns that often conflicts with long-term investment.

thai_meaning: จับต้องไม่ได้, นามธรรม
eng_definition: unable to be touched or measured; not having a physical presence
example_sentence: Trust is an ___ asset that organisations take years to build and seconds to destroy.

thai_meaning: ความสัมพันธ์ที่ส่งผลซึ่งกันและกัน
eng_definition: the way in which two or more things have an effect on each other; interaction
example_sentence: Understanding the ___ between monetary policy and fiscal strategy is essential for economic management.

thai_meaning: ที่รับรู้โดยสัญชาตญาณ, เข้าใจได้ทันที
eng_definition: based on instinct rather than conscious reasoning; easy to understand naturally
example_sentence: An ___ grasp of human motivation is often what distinguishes great leaders from merely competent ones.

thai_meaning: ท่วมท้น, ล้นหลาม
eng_definition: to overwhelm someone with things to be dealt with; to flood
example_sentence: The regulatory body was ___ with complaints after the product's dangerous defect was publicised.

thai_meaning: การประชดประชัน, สิ่งที่ขัดแย้งกับความคาดหวัง
eng_definition: the expression of meaning through language that normally signifies the opposite; a contradictory outcome
example_sentence: The ___ of a government promoting transparency while concealing data was not lost on commentators.

thai_meaning: ปฏิเสธไม่ได้, โต้แย้งไม่ได้
eng_definition: impossible to deny or disprove; unquestionable
example_sentence: The commission presented ___ evidence linking the corporation to the illegal dumping.

thai_meaning: ซับซ้อนเหมือนเขาวงกต, วกวน
eng_definition: extremely complicated; resembling a labyrinth in its complexity
example_sentence: The ___ procurement process deterred many small businesses from bidding for public contracts.

thai_meaning: ไม่มีชีวิตชีวา, น่าผิดหวัง
eng_definition: lacking in vitality, force, or conviction; not impressive; dull
example_sentence: The party's ___ election campaign reflected a leadership that had run out of ideas.

thai_meaning: เสรีนิยมสุดขีด, เชื่อในสิทธิเสรีภาพส่วนบุคคล
eng_definition: advocating minimal state intervention in the lives of citizens; favouring maximum individual freedom
example_sentence: A strictly ___ approach to regulation would leave consumers without meaningful protection.

thai_meaning: น่าสยดสยอง, เกี่ยวกับความตาย
eng_definition: disturbing and horrifying because of involvement with or depiction of death
example_sentence: The ___ details emerging from the investigation shocked even experienced criminal justice professionals.

thai_meaning: ขยายให้ใหญ่ขึ้น, เน้นความรุนแรง
eng_definition: to increase the size of something; to make something seem more significant than it is
example_sentence: Media coverage can both clarify and ___ public fears, with significant policy consequences.

thai_meaning: ทำให้เป็นกลาง, ลบล้าง
eng_definition: to make something ineffective by applying an opposite force; to render harmless
example_sentence: The antivenom is designed to ___ the toxins before they cause irreversible organ damage.

thai_meaning: ความเป็นกลาง, การมองโดยปราศจากอคติ
eng_definition: the quality of being uninfluenced by personal feelings or prejudice; impartiality
example_sentence: Claiming ___ does not guarantee it; all knowledge is produced from a particular vantage point.

thai_meaning: ทำลายให้หมดสิ้น, ลบออกจนไม่เหลือ
eng_definition: to destroy utterly; to wipe out all traces of something
example_sentence: A decade of conflict had ___ the social infrastructure that communities depended upon.

thai_meaning: เหมาะสมกับเวลา, มาพอดี
eng_definition: occurring at a favourable time; well-timed
example_sentence: The economic crisis provided an ___ moment to introduce reforms that would have been politically impossible otherwise.

thai_meaning: ดูเหมือนว่า, ภายนอกดูเหมือน
eng_definition: apparently or supposedly; used to suggest that the stated reason may not be the real one
example_sentence: The agency was ___ created to promote transparency but in practice served to obscure it.

thai_meaning: ตามใจในสิ่งที่ไม่ดี, ป้อนสิ่งที่คนต้องการโดยไม่คำนึงถึงศีลธรรม
eng_definition: to gratify or indulge an immoral or distasteful desire, especially for political gain
example_sentence: A politician who merely ___ to public fear cannot claim to be providing genuine leadership.

thai_meaning: สำนึกผิด, แสดงความสลดใจ
eng_definition: feeling or showing sorrow and regret for having done wrong
example_sentence: A ___ acknowledgment of institutional failure was seen as a prerequisite for rebuilding public trust.

thai_meaning: ผู้กระทำความผิด, ผู้ก่ออาชญากรรม
eng_definition: a person who carries out a harmful, illegal, or immoral act
example_sentence: International law establishes that ___ of crimes against humanity cannot claim immunity.

thai_meaning: ประจาน, โจมตีอย่างเปิดเผย
eng_definition: to attack or ridicule publicly; to expose to public mockery or criticism
example_sentence: The media ___ the official for a private remark that had been taken completely out of context.

thai_meaning: ปล้นสะดม, แสวงหาประโยชน์อย่างไม่เป็นธรรม
eng_definition: to steal goods using force, especially in war or disaster; to exploit a resource ruthlessly
example_sentence: Colonial economies were structured to systematically ___ the resources of conquered territories.

thai_meaning: ใคร่ครวญ, ไตร่ตรองอย่างถี่ถ้วน
eng_definition: to think carefully and seriously about something over a period of time
example_sentence: The committee took several weeks to ___ the implications of the scientific findings.

thai_meaning: คนรุ่นหลัง, อนาคต
eng_definition: all future generations of people; a person's descendants
example_sentence: Environmental legislation must be designed with ___ in mind, not just the present electoral cycle.

thai_meaning: ป้องกันไม่ให้เกิด, ทำให้เป็นไปไม่ได้
eng_definition: to prevent something from happening; to make something impossible
example_sentence: The evidence does not ___ the possibility that the decision was influenced by external pressure.

thai_meaning: เอารัดเอาเปรียบ, ล่าเหยื่อ
eng_definition: seeking to exploit or oppress others; relentlessly seeking prey
example_sentence: ___ lending practices targeted the most financially vulnerable members of the community.

thai_meaning: วางแผนล่วงหน้า, มีเจตนา
eng_definition: thought out or planned in advance; not spontaneous
example_sentence: The prosecution argued that the deception was ___ and not the result of poor judgment.

thai_meaning: สมมติเป็นฐาน, ถือว่าเป็นความจริงก่อน
eng_definition: to require as a prior condition; to assume something to be true without proof
example_sentence: The policy ___ a level of digital access that large segments of the population simply do not have.

thai_meaning: มหาศาล, น่าประหลาดใจในขนาด
eng_definition: remarkably or impressively large in size, quantity, or degree
example_sentence: She demonstrated a ___ capacity for work, producing three major publications in a single year.

thai_meaning: เพิ่มมากขึ้นอย่างรวดเร็ว, แพร่กระจาย
eng_definition: to increase rapidly in number; to cause something to grow or spread quickly
example_sentence: Misinformation will continue to ___ as long as digital platforms prioritise engagement over accuracy.

thai_meaning: เงื่อนไข, ข้อแม้
eng_definition: a condition or qualification attached to an agreement or statement
example_sentence: The funding was approved with the ___ that independent auditors would review expenditure quarterly.

thai_meaning: ความใกล้ชิด, ระยะใกล้
eng_definition: nearness in space, time, or relationship
example_sentence: Geographic ___ to major markets significantly reduces the cost of doing business.

thai_meaning: กวาดล้าง, ขจัดสิ่งไม่พึงประสงค์
eng_definition: to rid of unwanted elements by abrupt or violent means; to remove from an organisation
example_sentence: The new leadership moved to ___ officials linked to the previous administration from senior roles.

thai_meaning: สภาวะลำบากใจ, ความกลืนไม่เข้าคายไม่ออก
eng_definition: a state of perplexity or uncertainty about what to do in a difficult situation
example_sentence: Policymakers found themselves in a genuine ___ between protecting civil liberties and ensuring public safety.

thai_meaning: ผลกระทบที่ตามมา, ผลพวง
eng_definition: a consequence, especially when complex or unwelcome; a subdivision of a complex structure
example_sentence: The full ___ of the decision would not become apparent for several years.

thai_meaning: การโต้แย้ง, การหักล้าง
eng_definition: a refutation or contradicting of a claim, accusation, or argument
example_sentence: The research team published a detailed ___ of the claims made by the rival study.

thai_meaning: ลดทอนความซับซ้อน, มองแบบง่ายเกินไป
eng_definition: tending to present something in an oversimplified form; diminishing the complexity of something
example_sentence: Framing inequality as merely a matter of personal choice is dangerously ___.

thai_meaning: ละทิ้ง, สละ
eng_definition: to voluntarily cease to keep or claim; to give up
example_sentence: The incumbent agreed to ___ power only after international observers certified the election results.

thai_meaning: ประกาศสละ, ปฏิเสธอย่างเป็นทางการ
eng_definition: to formally declare one's abandonment of a claim, right, or possession
example_sentence: The accord required all parties to formally ___ the use of violence as a political instrument.

thai_meaning: ตำหนิ, ตำหนิด้วยความผิดหวัง
eng_definition: to express disapproval of someone; to address someone in such a way as to express disappointment
example_sentence: The committee's report ___ the ministry for systemic failures in protecting vulnerable children.

thai_meaning: การชดใช้คืน, การคืนสู่สภาพเดิม
eng_definition: the restoration of something lost or taken; compensation for loss or injury
example_sentence: Victims of the fraud were awarded ___ covering both direct losses and consequential damages.

thai_meaning: การเพิกถอน, การยกเลิกอย่างเป็นทางการ
eng_definition: the official cancellation of a decree, decision, or promise

thai_meaning: การแข่งขัน, ความเป็นคู่แข่ง
eng_definition: competition for the same objective or superiority in the same field
example_sentence: The ___ between the two research groups ultimately produced breakthroughs that neither could have achieved alone.

thai_meaning: ทำให้ศักดิ์สิทธิ์, รับรองทางศีลธรรม
eng_definition: to give official approval or a sacred character to; to make morally legitimate
example_sentence: No precedent, however old, can ___ a practice that is fundamentally unjust.

thai_meaning: หมิ่นประมาทด้วยวาจา, กล่าวร้าย
eng_definition: the oral defamation of a person's character by making false spoken statements
example_sentence: The candidate filed a civil suit alleging ___ after a series of false allegations were broadcast.

thai_meaning: อำนาจอธิปไตย, อำนาจสูงสุด
eng_definition: supreme power or authority; the authority of a state to govern itself
example_sentence: The principle of national ___ is increasingly in tension with the norms of global human rights law.

thai_meaning: ใช้สิ้นเปลืองอย่างสุรุ่ยสุร่าย, ทำให้สูญเปล่า
eng_definition: to waste money, time, or opportunity in a reckless or foolish way
example_sentence: The administration was accused of having ___ a historic opportunity for structural reform.

thai_meaning: มั่นคง, ไม่เปลี่ยนแปลง
eng_definition: resolutely firm and unwavering; not changing in commitment or purpose
example_sentence: Her ___ refusal to compromise on human rights principles cost her politically but earned lasting respect.

thai_meaning: บ่อนทำลาย, ล้มล้าง
eng_definition: to undermine the power and authority of an established system or institution
example_sentence: Money in politics can ___ the democratic process by giving outsized influence to the wealthy.

thai_meaning: ทำให้มัวหมอง, ทำให้ชื่อเสียงเสื่อม
eng_definition: to make or become less valuable or respected; to damage a reputation
example_sentence: The scandal ___ an institution that had built its reputation on decades of impartial service.

thai_meaning: เผด็จการเบ็ดเสร็จ, ควบคุมทุกอย่าง
eng_definition: relating to a system of government in which one power has complete control over public and private life
example_sentence: ___ regimes maintain power not only through force but through the manipulation of information.

thai_meaning: ทรยศ, ไม่น่าไว้ใจ
eng_definition: guilty of betrayal; not to be relied upon; presenting hidden dangers
example_sentence: The ___ terrain of international diplomacy requires patience, subtlety, and a long institutional memory.

thai_meaning: ไม่ยอมอ่อนข้อ, แน่วแน่
eng_definition: not giving way to pressure; refusing to change a position
example_sentence: An ___ commitment to truth-telling in public life is rare but essential to democratic health.

thai_meaning: ยึดอำนาจอย่างผิดกฎหมาย, แย่งชิง
eng_definition: to take a position of power unlawfully or by force; to supplant without right
example_sentence: The executive branch appeared to ___ legislative authority through a series of emergency decrees.

thai_meaning: ในอุดมคติ, เป็นเรื่องในฝัน
eng_definition: modelled on or aiming for an ideally perfect society; unrealistically idealistic

thai_meaning: รุนแรง, แสดงออกอย่างหนักแน่น
eng_definition: showing strong feeling; forceful, passionate, and intense

thai_meaning: ใส่ร้ายป้ายสี, ทำให้ดูเลวร้าย
eng_definition: to speak or write about in an abusively disparaging manner; to defame
example_sentence: Politicians who ___ minorities to mobilise their base exploit the worst instincts of democracy.

thai_meaning: มุ่งแก้แค้น, ต้องการทำร้ายผู้อื่น
eng_definition: having a strong desire to harm someone in return for a perceived grievance
example_sentence: The ___ prosecution of the journalist appeared designed to silence a critic rather than uphold the law.

thai_meaning: ลดลงทีละน้อย, เสื่อมถอย
eng_definition: to decrease in vigour, power, or extent; to decline gradually
example_sentence: Public enthusiasm for the reform began to ___ as its implementation stalled.

thai_meaning: เอาแน่เอานอนไม่ได้, ชอบคิดแปลกๆ
eng_definition: playfully quaint or fanciful; acting on impulse in a capricious way
example_sentence: The ___ narrative style of the essay masked a rigorous underlying analytical framework.

thai_meaning: ใช้อำนาจ, ถือครอง
eng_definition: to hold and use a weapon or tool; to have and be able to use power or influence
example_sentence: Those who ___ economic power must accept corresponding accountability for its social consequences.

thai_meaning: ความคลั่งไคล้ที่รุนแรง, การยึดมั่นสุดขีด
eng_definition: fanatical and uncompromising pursuit of ideals; extreme and intolerant devotion
example_sentence: Political ___ of any ideological stripe tends to produce worse outcomes than principled compromise.

thai_meaning: เกลียดชังอย่างสุดขีด
eng_definition: to detest utterly; to regard with extreme disgust and hatred
example_sentence: Many within the profession ___ the commodification of knowledge that has accompanied university privatisation.

thai_meaning: เข้าหาและพูดกับอย่างก้าวร้าว
eng_definition: to approach and address someone boldly or aggressively
example_sentence: Whistleblowers have been known to be ___ by private investigators hired to intimidate them.

thai_meaning: ทรมานใจ, ดิ้นรนกับการตัดสินใจ
eng_definition: to undergo great mental suffering; to struggle extremely in making a decision
example_sentence: Policymakers continue to ___ over the ethical trade-offs inherent in any surveillance framework.

thai_meaning: มิตรภาพ, ความสัมพันธ์ฉันมิตร
eng_definition: a friendly and peaceful relationship between people or countries
example_sentence: Decades of carefully cultivated ___ between the two nations could not survive the territorial dispute.

thai_meaning: ความเจ็บปวดอย่างรุนแรง, ความทุกข์ทรมาน
eng_definition: severe mental or physical pain or suffering
example_sentence: The committee heard testimony of extraordinary ___ from communities displaced without consultation.

thai_meaning: ฝ่อ, ลีบลง, เสื่อมถอย
eng_definition: to gradually decline in effectiveness or strength; to waste away through disuse
example_sentence: Democratic institutions begin to ___ when citizens disengage from civic participation.

thai_meaning: การลบหลู่ศาสนา, การดูหมิ่นสิ่งศักดิ์สิทธิ์
eng_definition: the act of speaking sacrilegiously about sacred things or persons; a statement that offends religious feeling
example_sentence: In some jurisdictions, ___ laws are still used to criminalise legitimate criticism of religious doctrine.

thai_meaning: ภาพล้อเลียน, การเกินจริงเพื่อล้อเลียน
eng_definition: an exaggerated representation of a person or thing for satirical effect; a distorted or absurd likeness
example_sentence: The media's ___ of the reform movement obscured the genuine grievances that motivated it.

thai_meaning: การเซ็นเซอร์, การปิดกั้นการแสดงออก
eng_definition: the suppression or prohibition of speech, publication, or other communication
example_sentence: State ___ of the internet is increasingly recognised as a violation of fundamental human rights.

thai_meaning: บังคับยึด, เกณฑ์ใช้โดยอำนาจ
eng_definition: to officially take possession of something for military or state purposes
example_sentence: Emergency laws allow the government to ___ private property during a declared national crisis.

thai_meaning: ฝ่าฝืน, ละเมิด
eng_definition: to act in defiance of a rule, treaty, or other controlling authority
example_sentence: The unilateral action was found to ___ several binding obligations under international treaty law.

thai_meaning: ระบบพรรคพวก, การเล่นพรรคเล่นพวก
eng_definition: the appointment of friends and associates to positions of authority without proper regard to their qualifications
example_sentence: ___ in public procurement has cost billions and systematically excluded competent but unconnected contractors.

thai_meaning: กล้าหาญไม่กลัวใคร, ไม่ท้อถอย
eng_definition: showing fearlessness and determination; not discouraged by obstacles or danger
example_sentence: Her ___ pursuit of justice through a decade of failed appeals eventually led to a landmark exoneration.

thai_meaning: การโกหก, การหลอกลวง
eng_definition: the action or practice of deceiving someone by misleading or lying
example_sentence: The commission found evidence of systematic ___ that had pervaded all levels of the organisation.

thai_meaning: ยกย่องเป็นเทพ, บูชาเกินควร
eng_definition: to worship as a god; to regard or treat someone with excessive or uncritical admiration

thai_meaning: ทำให้สกปรก, ทำลายสิ่งศักดิ์สิทธิ์
eng_definition: to treat a sacred place or thing with violent disrespect; to violate the sanctity of something
example_sentence: The deliberate destruction of cultural heritage sites is a war crime equivalent to the attempt to ___ a people's memory.

thai_meaning: ทำให้ไม่มั่นคง, บ่อนทำลายเสถียรภาพ
eng_definition: to make a country, government, or person unable to function effectively
example_sentence: Foreign interference designed to ___ democratic institutions represents a serious security threat.

thai_meaning: ระบอบเผด็จการ, การปกครองอย่างเบ็ดเสร็จ
eng_definition: a form of government in which one person possesses absolute power without effective checks
example_sentence: A ___ that relies on fear can only maintain itself by escalating violence against its own citizens.

thai_meaning: รุนแรงมาก, เร่งด่วนที่สุด
eng_definition: extremely serious or urgent; dreadful and causing extreme worry
example_sentence: The organisation issued a ___ warning about the consequences of inaction on climate-related risks.

thai_meaning: เปิดเผย, แจ้งให้ทราบ
eng_definition: to make previously unknown information known to others; to reveal
example_sentence: The company was compelled by the regulator to ___ all material risks to investors.

thai_meaning: ความขัดแย้ง, ความไม่ลงรอยกัน
eng_definition: disagreement or lack of harmony between people or groups; a harsh combination of sounds
example_sentence: Persistent ___ within the coalition eventually made coherent policymaking impossible.

thai_meaning: ถอนตัว, แยกออก
eng_definition: to separate or release from a connection; to withdraw from involvement
example_sentence: When citizens ___ from the political process, they cede power to those with the most to gain.

thai_meaning: ผู้เห็นต่าง, ผู้ต่อต้านรัฐบาล
eng_definition: a person who opposes official policy, especially that of an authoritarian state
example_sentence: The jailing of political ___ was condemned as a deliberate attempt to silence legitimate opposition.

thai_meaning: ร้ายแรงอย่างเห็นได้ชัด, ชัดเจนมาก
eng_definition: outstandingly bad; flagrant and shocking in its severity
example_sentence: The court characterised the procedural violations as ___ and ordered a full retrial.

thai_meaning: ทำให้ไร้พลัง, ทำให้อ่อนแรง
eng_definition: to cause someone to feel drained of energy or vitality; to weaken

thai_meaning: ผิดพลาด, เบี่ยงเบนจากหน้าที่
eng_definition: acting or tending to act contrary to expected or proper standards; straying from proper conduct

thai_meaning: หลีกเลี่ยง, หนีจากความรับผิดชอบ
eng_definition: to escape or avoid something by cleverness or deceit; to fail to deal with a question directly

thai_meaning: ลังเล, เริ่มสั่นคลอน
eng_definition: to lose strength or momentum; to speak or move in a hesitating way
example_sentence: When political will begins to ___, the most vulnerable populations are invariably the first to suffer.

thai_meaning: ความวุ่นวาย, สภาวะปั่นป่วน

example_sentence: The country was in a state of political ___ as old certainties dissolved and new forces emerged.

thai_meaning: ปลุกปั่น, กระตุ้นให้เกิดความวุ่นวาย

example_sentence: The broadcaster was accused of using inflammatory rhetoric to ___ division within an already fragile society.

thai_meaning: เกี่ยวกับกระบวนการทางกฎหมาย, ละเอียดและเป็นระบบ

example_sentence: The auditor's ___ examination of the accounts uncovered irregularities spanning nearly a decade.

thai_meaning: การทะเลาะวิวาทอย่างอึกทึก

thai_meaning: ความยิ่งใหญ่, ความโอ่อ่า

thai_meaning: โชคร้าย, อาภัพ
eng_definition: unfortunate and deserving of pity; beset by misfortune
example_sentence: The ___ official became the public face of a scandal he had done nothing to create.

thai_meaning: ผิดกฎหมาย, ต้องห้าม

thai_meaning: มีชื่อเสียงโด่งดัง, รุ่งโรจน์

thai_meaning: แรงกระตุ้น, แรงผลักดัน

example_sentence: The financial crisis provided the ___ for the most comprehensive overhaul of regulation in a generation.

thai_meaning: ไม่น่าเชื่อถือ, เป็นไปไม่ได้
eng_definition: not seeming reasonable or probable; failing to convince

thai_meaning: ทำให้ดูเหมือนมีความผิด, โยนความผิดให้

example_sentence: The recovered documents appeared to ___ several senior officials in the wider conspiracy.

thai_meaning: แทรกซึม, เจาะเข้าไป
eng_definition: to enter or gain access to an organisation or place gradually and covertly
example_sentence: Intelligence agencies alleged that hostile foreign actors had ___ the highest levels of government.

thai_meaning: ยั่วยุ, ปลุกปั่นความโกรธแค้น
eng_definition: tending to arouse anger, hostility, or passion; deliberately provocative

thai_meaning: เอาอกเอาใจเพื่อหวังประโยชน์
eng_definition: to bring oneself into favour with someone by flattering or trying to please them

thai_meaning: ความอยุติธรรม, การละเมิดสิทธิ์
eng_definition: lack of fairness or justice; a situation in which wrong treatment is received

thai_meaning: ความไม่ยอมรับ, ความแคบทางความคิด

thai_meaning: ประนีประนอมไม่ได้, ขัดแย้งอย่างเด็ดขาด

thai_meaning: เขตอำนาจ, อำนาจทางกฎหมาย
eng_definition: the official power to make legal decisions; the territory over which authority is exercised

thai_meaning: จุดไฟ, ปลุกความรู้สึก

thai_meaning: มรดก, สิ่งที่ทิ้งไว้ให้คนรุ่นหลัง
eng_definition: something handed down from one generation to the next; a lasting impact

thai_meaning: ไม่กระตือรือร้น, ยอมรับแบบไม่เต็มใจ

thai_meaning: ขนาด, ความยิ่งใหญ่
eng_definition: the great size, extent, or importance of something
example_sentence: The researchers had not anticipated the ___ of the behavioural changes their intervention would produce.

thai_meaning: จัดการ, บิดเบือนหรือควบคุม
eng_definition: to handle or control in a skilful way; to control or influence unfairly
example_sentence: Authoritarian governments routinely ___ public opinion by controlling the information environment.

thai_meaning: การพลีชีพ, ความทนทุกข์เพื่อหลักการ
eng_definition: the suffering and death of a martyr; the ordeal of suffering for a cause
example_sentence: Prosecuting the activist risked creating a ___ narrative that would amplify her message.

thai_meaning: การเกลียดชังผู้หญิง

example_sentence: Systemic ___ in hiring practices cannot be addressed without data collection and transparent reporting.

thai_meaning: ลวดลาย, ธีมหลัก

thai_meaning: จำนวนมากมาย, มวลชน

example_sentence: The reform must address a ___ of structural problems simultaneously rather than one at a time.

thai_meaning: เรื่องเล่า, กรอบการตีความเหตุการณ์
eng_definition: a spoken or written account; the framing of events that shapes how they are understood
example_sentence: Controlling the political ___ is often as important as controlling the policy itself.

thai_meaning: เป็นลางไม่ดี, น่าหวาดหวั่น
eng_definition: giving the impression that something bad or unpleasant is going to happen
example_sentence: The ___ silence from the negotiating room suggested that talks had collapsed.

thai_meaning: การละเว้น, สิ่งที่ขาดหายไป
eng_definition: the act of leaving something out; something not included that should have been
example_sentence: The deliberate ___ of key data from the official report constituted a form of institutional deception.

thai_meaning: จัดการอย่างเป็นระบบ, วางแผนซับซ้อน
eng_definition: to plan or coordinate the elements of a complex situation to produce a desired effect
example_sentence: The investigation revealed that the cover-up had been ___ at the highest levels of the department.

thai_meaning: ความเท่าเทียมกัน, ภาวะสมดุล
eng_definition: the state or condition of being equal, especially in status or pay
example_sentence: Achieving pay ___ between equivalent roles across genders remains an unfinished policy challenge.

thai_meaning: ระบบอุปถัมภ์, การสนับสนุนจากผู้มีอำนาจ
eng_definition: the support given by a patron; the power to make appointments to government jobs
example_sentence: Political ___ in appointments to regulatory bodies undermines the independence those bodies require.

thai_meaning: การทรยศหักหลัง, ความไม่ซื่อสัตย์
eng_definition: deceitfulness and untrustworthiness; the practice of breaking pledges
example_sentence: The reversal of the solemn commitment was condemned internationally as an act of ___.

thai_meaning: ทำให้ไม่สงบ, ทำให้กังวล
eng_definition: to make someone anxious or unsettled; to disturb the normal functioning of
example_sentence: New evidence that ___ the prevailing scientific consensus demands rigorous examination rather than dismissal.

thai_meaning: ความหลากหลาย, จำนวนมากกว่า
eng_definition: the state of being plural; a large number; a majority of votes without an absolute majority
example_sentence: A healthy democracy accommodates a ___ of voices rather than demanding ideological conformity.

thai_meaning: โอ้อวด, ทำท่าสำคัญตัวเอง
eng_definition: affectedly grand, solemn, or self-important in speech or manner
example_sentence: His ___ lecture on institutional integrity sat uneasily with his own conduct while in office.

thai_meaning: ลางสังหรณ์, สัญญาณบอกเหตุ
eng_definition: a sign or warning that a momentous or calamitous event is likely to happen
example_sentence: Early economic indicators were treated as ___ of a crisis that analysts had long predicted.

thai_meaning: มีพลังมาก, ได้ผลสูง
eng_definition: having great power, influence, or effect; producing a strong bodily or chemical effect
example_sentence: Disinformation is a particularly ___ weapon because it exploits existing social divisions.

thai_meaning: ปราบปราม, ทำให้สงบ
eng_definition: to put an end to rebellion or disorder by force; to suppress or extinguish
example_sentence: Riot police were deployed to ___ the unrest that had spread across five cities overnight.

thai_meaning: ทำให้สุดขั้ว, ปลุกปั่นให้รุนแรง
eng_definition: to cause someone to adopt extreme political, social, or religious views
example_sentence: Research suggests that social isolation and economic marginalisation can ___ individuals susceptible to extremist messaging.

thai_meaning: ความขมขื่นแค้นเคือง, ความเจ็บแค้นที่คงอยู่นาน
eng_definition: bitterness or resentfulness, especially when long-standing
example_sentence: Decades of perceived injustice had hardened into a ___ that no political gesture could quickly dissolve.

thai_meaning: ทำลายราบคาบ, รื้อถอน
eng_definition: to completely destroy a building, town, or other settlement
example_sentence: The historic quarter was ___ to make way for a development that promised economic revitalisation.

thai_meaning: ไม่ลดละ, ไม่หยุดยั้ง
eng_definition: oppressively constant; never stopping or weakening; harsh and inflexible
example_sentence: Only ___ pressure from civil society eventually forced the government to publish the suppressed report.

thai_meaning: ความสำนึกผิด, ความเสียใจ
eng_definition: deep regret or guilt for a wrong committed; a sense of deep moral responsibility
example_sentence: Genuine ___ requires more than a public apology; it demands structural change.

thai_meaning: การแก้แค้น, การตอบโต้
eng_definition: an act of retaliation; injury inflicted in return for an injury received
example_sentence: Witnesses were reluctant to testify for fear of economic ___ from those they accused.

thai_meaning: ด่าว่าอย่างรุนแรง, ดูถูกอย่างเปิดเผย
eng_definition: to criticise someone in an abusive or contemptuous way; to assail with offensive language
example_sentence: Those who challenge established orthodoxies are often ___ before their ideas are eventually accepted.

thai_meaning: มีประโยชน์ต่อร่างกายหรือจิตใจ, เป็นบทเรียนที่ดี
eng_definition: producing a good effect; beneficial, especially as a lesson from a difficult experience
example_sentence: The failure proved a ___ reminder that arrogance in the face of complexity invites catastrophe.

thai_meaning: เย้ยหยัน, ดูถูกแฝงอารมณ์ขัน
eng_definition: grimly mocking or cynical; showing scorn or contempt in a humorous way
example_sentence: The commentator's ___ observation about the ministerial press release landed with devastating effect.

thai_meaning: ตำหนิรุนแรง, คมคายเจ็บปวด
eng_definition: witheringly scornful; bitterly critical or severe
example_sentence: The independent review delivered a ___ verdict on five years of failed institutional governance.

thai_meaning: รอบคอบละเอียดถี่ถ้วน, ยึดมั่นในหลักการ
eng_definition: diligent, thorough, and extremely attentive to accuracy and morality
example_sentence: ___ documentation of the chain of evidence is essential in any proceeding that may be legally contested.

thai_meaning: บ่งบอกความชั่วร้าย, น่าสงสัย
eng_definition: suggesting or threatening evil; giving the impression that something harmful is occurring
example_sentence: The timing of the announcement had a ___ convenience that journalists were quick to note.

thai_meaning: จริงจัง, เคร่งขรึม
eng_definition: formal and dignified in manner; characterised by deep sincerity
example_sentence: The committee made a ___ pledge to publish all findings regardless of where they led.

thai_meaning: การใช้เหตุผลที่ดูดีแต่หลอกลวง
eng_definition: the use of clever but false arguments with the intention to deceive
example_sentence: The minister's defence was widely condemned as ___ that could not withstand serious scrutiny.

thai_meaning: เสียงดังหยาบ, ยืนกรานอย่างรุนแรง
eng_definition: loud and harsh; presenting views in an excessively forceful way
example_sentence: A ___ tone in advocacy can alienate those who need to be persuaded rather than simply rallied.

thai_meaning: เอาชนะ, ฝ่าฟันอุปสรรค
eng_definition: to overcome a difficulty or challenge; to stand or be placed on top of
example_sentence: Effective multilateralism can ___ even the most intractable disputes when all parties are committed.

thai_meaning: ไม่ชอบพูด, เงียบขรึม
eng_definition: reserved or uncommunicative in speech; saying little
example_sentence: The ___ diplomat communicated more through strategic silence than many colleagues did through eloquent speeches.

thai_meaning: ลังเล, ไม่แน่นอน
eng_definition: not certain or fixed; done with caution and without confidence
example_sentence: Early ___ conclusions from the trial data have been received with cautious optimism by the scientific community.

thai_meaning: ความกลัวและลังเล, ความวิตกกังวล
eng_definition: a feeling of fear or agitation about something that may happen
example_sentence: Officials approached the review with considerable ___, aware of what a full examination might reveal.

thai_meaning: ความยากลำบาก, การทดสอบที่รุนแรง
eng_definition: a cause of great trouble or suffering; severe hardship or distress
example_sentence: Communities that endure shared ___ often emerge with stronger social bonds than those untested by adversity.

thai_meaning: ก้าวร้าว, ชอบต่อสู้
eng_definition: eager or quick to argue or fight; aggressively defiant
example_sentence: The witness's ___ demeanour under cross-examination undermined the credibility his testimony might otherwise have carried.

thai_meaning: การปกครองแบบทรราช, การใช้อำนาจกดขี่
eng_definition: cruel and oppressive government or rule; arbitrary or unrestrained exercise of power
example_sentence: The framers of the constitution were preoccupied above all with preventing any recurrence of executive ___.

thai_meaning: ยึดถือไม่ได้, พิสูจน์ไม่ได้
eng_definition: not able to be maintained or defended against attack or objection; indefensible
example_sentence: The position became ___ once the contrary evidence had been published in peer-reviewed journals.

thai_meaning: ไร้สาระ, จืดชืด
eng_definition: offering nothing that is stimulating or challenging; dull and uninteresting
example_sentence: The press release offered ___ corporate platitudes in place of the substantive commitments that stakeholders demanded.

thai_meaning: ที่รับสินบนได้, โลภเห็นแก่เงิน
eng_definition: susceptible to bribery; motivated chiefly by monetary gain
example_sentence: A ___ judiciary renders all other institutions of accountability effectively worthless.

thai_meaning: การแก้แค้น, การตอบโต้
eng_definition: punishment inflicted or retribution exacted for an injury or wrong
example_sentence: Policies motivated by ___ rather than justice rarely produce the durable peace they promise.

thai_meaning: ความตื่นตัว, การระวังภัย
eng_definition: the action or state of keeping careful watch for possible danger or difficulties
example_sentence: Democratic freedoms require constant ___ against the gradual accumulation of unchecked executive power.

thai_meaning: ร้ายแรง, รุนแรงมากและแพร่เชื้อได้ง่าย
eng_definition: extremely severe or harmful in its effects; bitterly hostile
example_sentence: A particularly ___ strain of misinformation spread faster than corrective information could counter it.

thai_meaning: ที่รู้สึกจากส่วนลึก, สัญชาตญาณ
eng_definition: relating to deep inward feelings rather than intellect; instinctive and deeply felt
example_sentence: The images provoked a ___ public reaction that proved impossible for legislators to ignore.

thai_meaning: เสียงดัง, เรียกร้องอย่างแข็งกร้าว
eng_definition: expressing or characterised by vehement opinions in a loud and forceful way
example_sentence: A ___ minority within the profession had opposed the reform from the outset, loudly and persistently.

thai_meaning: ระวัง, ไม่ไว้วางใจ
eng_definition: feeling or showing caution about possible dangers or problems
example_sentence: Investors remain ___ of markets where the rule of law is inconsistently applied.

thai_meaning: ดื้อ, เดินหลงทาง
eng_definition: difficult to control; following an erratic path; not conforming to expected norms
example_sentence: A ___ institution that resists all attempts at reform may ultimately require external intervention.

thai_meaning: ยกเลิก, เพิกถอน (กฎหมายหรือข้อตกลง)
eng_definition: to repeal or do away with a law, right, or formal agreement
example_sentence: The new administration intends to abrogate the outdated trade policies.

thai_meaning: รู้จักพอประมาณ, 절제하는 (โดยเฉพาะเรื่องอาหารและเครื่องดื่ม)
eng_definition: indulging only very moderately in something, especially food and drink
example_sentence: His abstemious lifestyle contributed to his remarkable health in old age.

thai_meaning: แรเงา, เค้าโครง, อธิบายคร่าวๆ
eng_definition: to report or represent in outline; to indicate faintly
example_sentence: The president adumbrated a plan for healthcare reform during his speech.

thai_meaning: สิ่งที่น่ารังเกียจ, สิ่งที่ถูกสาปแช่ง
eng_definition: something or someone that one vehemently dislikes
example_sentence: The idea of raising taxes is anathema to many conservative voters.

thai_meaning: โบราณมาก, ล้าสมัยสุดๆ (ราวกับอยู่ก่อนยุคน้ำท่วมโลก)
eng_definition: ridiculously old-fashioned; belonging to the time before the biblical Flood
example_sentence: They still use antediluvian equipment in that laboratory.

thai_meaning: จุดสูงสุดของความสำเร็จ, การยกย่องให้เป็นเทพเจ้า
eng_definition: the highest point in the development of something; culmination or climax
example_sentence: His appearance on the cover of the magazine was the apotheosis of his career.

thai_meaning: ความหยาบกระด้าง (กิริยาหรือน้ำเสียง), ความลำบาก
eng_definition: harshness of tone or manner
example_sentence: He pointed out, with some asperity, that the deadline had already passed.

thai_meaning: ยืนยันอย่างหนักแน่น
eng_definition: to declare earnestly or solemnly; affirm positively
example_sentence: The witness continued to asseverate his innocence despite the evidence.

thai_meaning: ขยันขันแข็ง, พากเพียรอย่างมาก
eng_definition: showing great care and perseverance
example_sentence: She was assiduous in pointing out every mistake I made.

thai_meaning: เป็นมงคล, มีฤกษ์ดี, มีแนวโน้มว่าจะสำเร็จ
eng_definition: conducive to success; favorable
example_sentence: The clear sky provided an auspicious beginning for the long journey.

thai_meaning: ความโลภ, ความอยากได้ทรัพย์สินอย่างมาก
eng_definition: extreme greed for wealth or material gain
example_sentence: Corporate avarice was blamed for the financial crisis.

thai_meaning: น่าเบื่อ, ธรรมดาเกินไป, ซ้ำซาก
eng_definition: so lacking in originality as to be obvious and boring
example_sentence: The pop song's lyrics were so banal that I predicted every rhyme.

thai_meaning: หลอกลวง, ดึงดูดใจด้วยเล่ห์เหลี่ยม
eng_definition: charm or enchant (someone), sometimes in a deceptive way
example_sentence: He used his good looks to beguile her into trusting him.

thai_meaning: คำหวาน, การประจบสอพลอเพื่อโน้มน้าวใจ
eng_definition: a flattering or pleasing statement or action used to persuade someone gently
example_sentence: Despite his blandishments, she refused to invest in his dubious scheme.

thai_meaning: ใช้คำพูดใหญ่โตเกินจริงแต่ไม่มีสาระ
eng_definition: high-sounding but with little meaning; inflated
example_sentence: The politician delivered a bombastic speech that impressed no one.

thai_meaning: หยาบคาย, ไร้มารยาท
eng_definition: rough and bad-mannered; coarse
example_sentence: His boorish behavior at the dinner party embarrassed his wife.

thai_meaning: เกี่ยวกับชนบท, สงบสุขแบบชนบท
eng_definition: relating to the pleasant aspects of the countryside and country life
example_sentence: They bought a bucolic little cottage in the middle of nowhere.

thai_meaning: เติบโตอย่างรวดเร็ว, แตกหน่อ
eng_definition: begin to grow or increase rapidly; flourish
example_sentence: The city's suburbs have burgeoned in recent years.

thai_meaning: เสียงที่ดังหนวกหูและไม่ประสานกัน
eng_definition: a harsh discordant mixture of sounds
example_sentence: A cacophony of car horns woke her up in the middle of the night.

thai_meaning: การใส่ร้ายป้ายสี
eng_definition: the making of false and defamatory statements about someone in order to damage their reputation
example_sentence: He was the target of a vicious calumny designed to ruin his political career.

thai_meaning: เอาแน่เอานอนไม่ได้, เปลี่ยนแปลงง่าย
eng_definition: given to sudden and unaccountable changes of mood or behavior
example_sentence: The capricious weather ruined our plans for a picnic.

thai_meaning: ชอบจับผิด, จู้จี้
eng_definition: tending to find fault or raise petty objections
example_sentence: The captious reviewer criticized every minor detail of the novel.

thai_meaning: ตำหนิอย่างรุนแรง
eng_definition: reprimand (someone) severely
example_sentence: The principal castigated the students for their disruptive behavior.

thai_meaning: ตัวเร่งปฏิกิริยา, สิ่งที่ทำให้เกิดการเปลี่ยนแปลง
eng_definition: a person or thing that precipitates an event
example_sentence: The protests served as a catalyst for political reform.

thai_meaning: กัดกร่อน, (คำพูด) ถากถางอย่างรุนแรง
eng_definition: able to burn or corrode organic tissue by chemical action; sarcastic in a scathing and bitter way
example_sentence: Her caustic remarks made her unpopular among her colleagues.

thai_meaning: ความรวดเร็ว, ความฉับไว
eng_definition: swiftness of movement
example_sentence: The journalist wrote his report with great celerity to meet the deadline.

thai_meaning: เพ้อฝัน, ไม่มีทางเป็นจริง
eng_definition: hoped for but illusory or impossible to achieve
example_sentence: His plans to build a city on Mars seemed completely chimerical.

thai_meaning: การพูดอ้อมค้อม
eng_definition: the use of many words where fewer would do, especially in a deliberate attempt to be vague or evasive
example_sentence: When asked about the missing funds, the manager responded with endless circumlocution.

thai_meaning: มีเหตุผลและน่าเชื่อถือมาก
eng_definition: clear, logical, and convincing
example_sentence: She presented a cogent argument for increasing the education budget.

thai_meaning: ได้สัดส่วนกัน, เหมาะสมกับ
eng_definition: corresponding in size or degree; in proportion
example_sentence: Salary will be commensurate with age and experience.

thai_meaning: หนังสือรวบรวมข้อมูล, บทสรุปที่ครอบคลุม
eng_definition: a collection of concise but detailed information about a particular subject
example_sentence: He published a compendium of folk tales from around the world.

thai_meaning: โอนอ่อนผ่อนตาม, เต็มใจทำให้ผู้อื่นพอใจ
eng_definition: willing to please others; obliging; agreeable
example_sentence: The complaisant staff made our stay at the hotel highly enjoyable.

thai_meaning: ประนีประนอม, ซึ่งเป็นการไกล่เกลี่ย
eng_definition: intended or likely to placate or pacify
example_sentence: He adopted a conciliatory tone in hopes of resolving the dispute peacefully.

thai_meaning: ทำให้สับสนงุนงง, พิสูจน์ว่าผิด
eng_definition: cause surprise or confusion in (someone), especially by acting against their expectations
example_sentence: The complicated instructions served only to confound the students.

thai_meaning: ผู้เชี่ยวชาญ (โดยเฉพาะศิลปะหรืออาหาร)
eng_definition: an expert judge in matters of taste
example_sentence: As a wine connoisseur, he could easily identify the vineyard and vintage.

thai_meaning: ชอบโต้เถียง, ซึ่งก่อให้เกิดความขัดแย้ง
eng_definition: causing or likely to cause an argument; controversial
example_sentence: Abortion has always been a highly contentious issue.

thai_meaning: สำนึกผิดอย่างสุดซึ้ง
eng_definition: feeling or expressing remorse or penitence; affected by guilt
example_sentence: He wrote a contrite letter apologizing for his reckless behavior.

thai_meaning: ดื้อรั้น, ต่อต้านอำนาจ
eng_definition: stubbornly or willfully disobedient to authority
example_sentence: The judge warned the contumacious defendant that he would be held in contempt of court.

thai_meaning: ปริศนา, ปัญหาที่แก้ได้ยาก
eng_definition: a confusing and difficult problem or question
example_sentence: Arranging childcare over the school holidays can be quite a conundrum for working parents.

thai_meaning: ซับซ้อน, ยุ่งเหยิงเข้าใจยาก
eng_definition: extremely complex and difficult to follow
example_sentence: The movie's plot was so convoluted that I lost track of what was happening.

thai_meaning: ขี้ขลาดตาขาว
eng_definition: contemptibly lacking in courage; cowardly
example_sentence: A craven act of terrorism took the lives of dozens of innocent people.

thai_meaning: หูเบา, เชื่อคนง่าย
eng_definition: having or showing too great a readiness to believe things
example_sentence: Scammers often target credulous elderly people.

thai_meaning: ความขาดแคลนอย่างหนัก
eng_definition: a scarcity or lack of something
example_sentence: There is a severe dearth of evidence to support his wild claims.

thai_meaning: ความล้มเหลวอย่างสิ้นเชิง, ความหายนะ
eng_definition: a sudden and ignominious failure; a fiasco
example_sentence: The launch of the new software product was a complete debacle.

thai_meaning: ความมีมารยาท, ความเหมาะสม
eng_definition: behavior in keeping with good taste and propriety
example_sentence: You are expected to conduct yourself with decorum during the graduation ceremony.

thai_meaning: เป็นอันตราย, ให้โทษ
eng_definition: causing harm or damage
example_sentence: Divorce is assumed to have deleterious effects on children.

thai_meaning: นักการเมืองที่ปลุกปั่นฝูงชนด้วยอารมณ์
eng_definition: a political leader who seeks support by appealing to the desires and prejudices of ordinary people rather than by using rational argument
example_sentence: The senator was widely criticized as a demagogue who preyed on public fears.

thai_meaning: ดูถูก, ด้อยค่า
eng_definition: criticize unfairly; disparage
example_sentence: There is a tendency to denigrate the poor and blame them for their circumstances.

thai_meaning: ทำให้แห้งสนิท, ไร้ชีวิตชีวา
eng_definition: remove the moisture from (something); cause to become completely dry
example_sentence: The long drought had severely desiccated the crops.

thai_meaning: บางเบาจนมองทะลุได้ (ผืนผ้า)
eng_definition: (especially of fabric) light, delicate, and translucent
example_sentence: The dancer wore a diaphanous silk dress that floated gracefully as she moved.

thai_meaning: การวิจารณ์อย่างรุนแรง, การด่าทอยืดยาว
eng_definition: a forceful and bitter verbal attack against someone or something
example_sentence: The CEO's unexpected diatribe against the board of directors shocked everyone.

thai_meaning: ขาดความมั่นใจ, ประหม่า
eng_definition: modest or shy because of a lack of self-confidence
example_sentence: He was a diffident young man who rarely spoke up in meetings.

thai_meaning: ชักช้า, ถ่วงเวลา
eng_definition: slow to act; intended to cause delay
example_sentence: The government has been dilatory in implementing the new environmental regulations.

thai_meaning: ผู้รักศิลปะหรือความรู้แบบผิวเผิน, มือสมัครเล่น
eng_definition: a person who cultivates an area of interest, such as the arts, without real commitment or knowledge
example_sentence: He's a bit of a dilettante when it comes to playing the piano.

thai_meaning: เพลงไว้อาลัย, เพลงเศร้า
eng_definition: a lament for the dead, especially one forming part of a funeral rite
example_sentence: The wind howled through the trees like a mournful dirge.

thai_meaning: ทำให้เข้าใจถูก, แก้ความเข้าใจผิด
eng_definition: persuade (someone) that an idea or belief is mistaken
example_sentence: Let me disabuse you of the notion that I am going to pay for this.

thai_meaning: ทำให้กระอักกระอ่วนใจ, ทำให้พ่ายแพ้
eng_definition: make (someone) feel uneasy or embarrassed
example_sentence: The reporter's probing questions seemed to discomfit the politician.

thai_meaning: ขัดแย้งกัน, ไม่ประสานกัน (เสียง)
eng_definition: disagreeing or incongruous; harsh and jarring because of a lack of harmony
example_sentence: The orchestra played a series of discordant notes during the avant-garde performance.

thai_meaning: ไม่จริงใจ, เสแสร้งว่าไม่รู้
eng_definition: not candid or sincere, typically by pretending that one knows less about something than one really does
example_sentence: It was disingenuous of her to claim she had no financial interest in the case.

thai_meaning: ดูถูก, ทำให้เสื่อมเสีย
eng_definition: regard or represent as being of little worth
example_sentence: He never missed an opportunity to disparage his competitors.

thai_meaning: แตกต่างกันอย่างสิ้นเชิง, ไม่สามารถนำมาเปรียบเทียบกันได้
eng_definition: essentially different in kind; not allowing comparison
example_sentence: They inhabit disparate worlds of wealth and poverty.

thai_meaning: ซ่อนความรู้สึกหรือเจตนาที่แท้จริง, เสแสร้ง
eng_definition: conceal one's true motives, feelings, or beliefs
example_sentence: An honest person will not dissemble their true intentions.

thai_meaning: ความไม่ประสานกัน, ความขัดแย้งกัน
eng_definition: a tension or clash resulting from the combination of two disharmonious or unsuitable elements
example_sentence: Cognitive dissonance occurs when your beliefs conflict with your actions.

thai_meaning: ดื้อรั้นในความเชื่อของตน, ถือทิฐิ
eng_definition: inclined to lay down principles as incontrovertibly true
example_sentence: You can't be dogmatic in matters of art interpretation.

thai_meaning: ร่าเริงแจ่มใส, กระตือรือร้นล้นหลาม
eng_definition: cheerful and full of energy
example_sentence: She sounded ebullient about her new job prospects.

thai_meaning: เลือกสรรจากหลายแหล่ง, ผสมผสาน
eng_definition: deriving ideas, style, or taste from a broad and diverse range of sources
example_sentence: The museum's collection is highly eclectic, ranging from ancient artifacts to modern pop art.

thai_meaning: ประสิทธิภาพ, ความสามารถในการให้ผลลัพธ์ที่ต้องการ
eng_definition: the ability to produce a desired or intended result
example_sentence: Clinical trials proved the efficacy of the new vaccine.

thai_meaning: ความหน้าด้าน, ความทะลึ่ง
eng_definition: insolent or impertinent behavior
example_sentence: One juror had the effrontery to challenge the judge's ruling.

thai_meaning: บทกวีไว้อาลัย
eng_definition: a poem of serious reflection, typically a lament for the dead
example_sentence: He composed a beautiful elegy in memory of his late grandmother.

thai_meaning: ที่ได้จากการทดลองหรือการสังเกต (ไม่ใช่อิงทฤษฎีอย่างเดียว)
eng_definition: based on, concerned with, or verifiable by observation or experience rather than theory or pure logic
example_sentence: They provided empirical evidence to support their controversial hypothesis.

thai_meaning: ทำให้หมดแรง, บั่นทอนกำลัง
eng_definition: cause (someone) to feel drained of energy or vitality; weaken
example_sentence: The hot, humid weather enervated the hikers.

thai_meaning: ก่อให้เกิด, นำมาซึ่ง
eng_definition: cause or give rise to (a feeling, situation, or condition)
example_sentence: The issue has engendered a considerable amount of debate.

thai_meaning: ความรู้สึกเบื่อหน่ายอย่างมาก, ความเซ็ง
eng_definition: a feeling of listlessness and dissatisfaction arising from a lack of occupation or excitement
example_sentence: He succumbed to ennui and despair during the long, inactive winter.

thai_meaning: ตัวอย่างที่สมบูรณ์แบบ, แบบฉบับ
eng_definition: a person or thing that is a perfect example of a particular quality or type
example_sentence: Even in times of extreme stress, she was the epitome of grace and calm.

thai_meaning: ความสงบเยือกเย็น (โดยเฉพาะในสถานการณ์ที่ตึงเครียด)
eng_definition: mental calmness, composure, and evenness of temper, especially in a difficult situation
example_sentence: She accepted both her victories and her defeats with remarkable equanimity.

thai_meaning: พูดกำกวมเพื่อหลีกเลี่ยงการผูกมัดหรือซ่อนความจริง
eng_definition: use ambiguous language so as to conceal the truth or avoid committing oneself
example_sentence: When asked if he planned to raise taxes, the governor began to equivocate.

thai_meaning: มีความรู้กว้างขวาง, เป็นนักวิชาการ
eng_definition: having or showing great knowledge or learning
example_sentence: The professor is highly respected for his erudite publications on ancient history.

thai_meaning: เข้าใจได้เฉพาะกลุ่มคนเล็กๆ, ลี้ลับ
eng_definition: intended for or likely to be understood by only a small number of people with a specialized knowledge or interest
example_sentence: He has an esoteric collection of antique mechanical puzzles.

thai_meaning: จางหายไปอย่างรวดเร็ว, ไม่จีรัง
eng_definition: soon passing out of sight, memory, or existence; quickly fading or disappearing
example_sentence: The shimmering rainbow was an evanescent phenomenon that vanished in seconds.

thai_meaning: ทำให้แย่ลง, ทำให้รุนแรงขึ้น
eng_definition: make (a problem, bad situation, or negative feeling) worse
example_sentence: Scratching an insect bite will only exacerbate the itching.

thai_meaning: พิสูจน์ว่าไม่มีความผิด, พ้นข้อกล่าวหา
eng_definition: show or declare that (someone) is not guilty of wrongdoing
example_sentence: The article exculpated the mayor of all charges related to the scandal.

thai_meaning: แย่มาก, น่ารังเกียจที่สุด
eng_definition: extremely bad or unpleasant
example_sentence: We were served an execrable meal at the poorly reviewed restaurant.

thai_meaning: การอธิบายหรือตีความอย่างละเอียด (มักใช้กับคัมภีร์ทางศาสนา)
eng_definition: critical explanation or interpretation of a text, especially of scripture
example_sentence: The theologian's exegesis of the biblical passage was widely studied.

thai_meaning: ชดใช้ความผิด, ไถ่บาป
eng_definition: atone for (guilt or sin)
synonym: atone for, make amends for, redeem
example_sentence: To expiate his past crimes, he dedicated his life to helping the poor.

thai_meaning: ถอนรากถอนโคน, ทำลายจนสิ้นซาก
eng_definition: root out and destroy completely
example_sentence: The new government tried to extirpate all signs of the previous regime.

thai_meaning: พูดเล่นในเวลาที่ไม่สมควร, ตลกฝืด
eng_definition: treating serious issues with deliberately inappropriate humor; flippant
example_sentence: Please stop making facetious remarks during this important meeting.

thai_meaning: ซึ่งมีเหตุผลผิดพลาด, หลอกลวง
eng_definition: based on a mistaken belief
example_sentence: His argument is based on a completely fallacious assumption.

thai_meaning: โง่เขลา, ไร้สาระอย่างมาก
eng_definition: silly and pointless
example_sentence: The movie was ruined by the inclusion of a fatuous romance subplot.

thai_meaning: ประจบสอพลออย่างเห็นได้ชัด
eng_definition: displaying exaggerated flattery or affection; obsequious
example_sentence: The celebrity was surrounded by fawning admirers wherever he went.

thai_meaning: เหมาะสมเจาะจง, เลือกใช้คำได้ดี
eng_definition: well chosen or suited to the circumstances
example_sentence: The chairman's felicitous opening remarks set a positive tone for the conference.

thai_meaning: เย้ยหยัน, ฝ่าฝืนอย่างเปิดเผย (เช่น กฎหมาย)
eng_definition: openly disregard (a rule, law or convention)
example_sentence: Many motorcyclists openly flout the law by not wearing helmets.

thai_meaning: ปลุกปั่นให้เกิดความรุนแรงหรือความขัดแย้ง
eng_definition: instigate or stir up (an undesirable or violent sentiment or course of action)
example_sentence: They were accused of trying to foment a rebellion against the state.

thai_meaning: ป้องกันล่วงหน้า, ดักทาง
eng_definition: prevent or obstruct (an anticipated event or action) by taking action ahead of time
example_sentence: Vitamins may help to forestall many age-related diseases.

thai_meaning: หงุดหงิดง่าย, ควบคุมยาก
eng_definition: (typically of children) irritable and quarrelsome; (of a group or organization) difficult to control; unruly
example_sentence: The fractious crowd demanded an immediate explanation from the mayor.

thai_meaning: ประณามอย่างรุนแรง, ระเบิดอารมณ์
eng_definition: express vehement protest
example_sentence: He used his newspaper column to fulminate against the corruption in local government.

thai_meaning: ช่างพูด, พูดมากในเรื่องไร้สาระ
eng_definition: excessively talkative, especially on trivial matters
example_sentence: I found myself seated next to a terribly garrulous old man on the train.

thai_meaning: ใช้ถ้อยคำหรูหราโอ้อวด (เพื่อให้ดูฉลาดหรือสำคัญ)
eng_definition: pompous or extravagant in language, style, or manner, especially in a way that is intended to impress
example_sentence: The politician's grandiloquent speeches eventually alienated the working-class voters.

thai_meaning: ชอบเข้าสังคม, อยู่รวมกันเป็นฝูง
eng_definition: (of a person) fond of company; sociable
example_sentence: Dolphins are highly gregarious animals that live in complex social groups.

thai_meaning: ซ้ำซาก, เก่าคร่ำคร่า (เพราะใช้บ่อยเกินไป)
eng_definition: lacking significance through having been overused; unoriginal and trite
example_sentence: The movie's plot was so hackneyed that I could predict the ending within the first ten minutes.

thai_meaning: สงบสุข, รุ่งเรือง (มักใช้พูดถึงอดีตที่งดงาม)
eng_definition: denoting a period of time in the past that was idyllically happy and peaceful
example_sentence: She often reminisced about the halcyon days of her youth before the war began.

thai_meaning: การด่าทอหรือสั่งสอนอย่างรุนแรงและยืดยาว
eng_definition: a lengthy and aggressive speech
example_sentence: The manager delivered a fiery harangue to the team after their dismal performance.

thai_meaning: ความเป็นผู้นำ, อำนาจครอบงำ (โดยเฉพาะทางการเมืองหรือวัฒนธรรม)
eng_definition: leadership or dominance, especially by one country or social group over others
example_sentence: The country fought hard to maintain its economic hegemony in the region.

thai_meaning: ปิดผนึกอย่างมิดชิด, ตัดขาดจากโลกภายนอก
eng_definition: complete and airtight; insulated or protected from outside influences
example_sentence: The strange cult lived a hermetic existence, completely cut off from modern society.

thai_meaning: นอกรีต, ซึ่งผิดไปจากความเชื่อที่เป็นที่ยอมรับ
eng_definition: not conforming with accepted or orthodox standards or beliefs
example_sentence: His heterodox views on economic policy made him unpopular with conservative politicians.

thai_meaning: เล่นละคร, เสแสร้งเกินจริงเพื่อเรียกร้องความสนใจ
eng_definition: overly theatrical or melodramatic in character or style
example_sentence: Her histrionic reaction to the minor scratch on her car annoyed everyone.

thai_meaning: ความหยิ่งจองหอง, ความมั่นใจในตัวเองมากเกินไป
eng_definition: excessive pride or self-confidence
example_sentence: It was hubris that led the CEO to believe his company could never fail.

thai_meaning: ผู้ต่อต้านหรือทำลายความเชื่อ/ประเพณีดั้งเดิม
eng_definition: a person who attacks cherished beliefs or institutions
example_sentence: As an iconoclast, the artist constantly challenged the established norms of the art world.

thai_meaning: ลักษณะเฉพาะตัว, นิสัยแปลกๆ ส่วนบุคคล
eng_definition: a mode of behavior or way of thought peculiar to an individual
example_sentence: One of his little idiosyncrasies is always eating the crust of the pizza first.

thai_meaning: น่าอับอายขายหน้า, น่าเสื่อมเสียเกียรติ
eng_definition: deserving or causing public disgrace or shame
example_sentence: The general was forced into an ignominious retreat after his army was outmaneuvered.

thai_meaning: สถานการณ์ที่ยุ่งเหยิงและน่าอับอาย, ความพัวพันที่ซับซ้อน
eng_definition: an extremely confused, complicated, or embarrassing situation
example_sentence: The government managed to survive the political imbroglio caused by the leaked emails.

thai_meaning: ไม่สามารถเปลี่ยนแปลงได้, ตายตัว
eng_definition: unchanging over time or unable to be changed
example_sentence: The laws of physics are generally considered to be immutable.

thai_meaning: ไร้อารมณ์, ไม่แสดงความรู้สึก
eng_definition: not feeling or showing emotion
example_sentence: The defendant remained impassive as the judge read the guilty verdict.

thai_meaning: ยากจน, ไม่มีเงิน
eng_definition: having little or no money
example_sentence: The impecunious student relied on scholarships and part-time jobs to survive.

thai_meaning: วางอำนาจ, สั่งการอย่างเผด็จการ
eng_definition: assuming power or authority without justification; arrogant and domineering
example_sentence: The imperious manager demanded that everyone work through the weekend.

thai_meaning: ใจเย็นมาก, ไม่หวั่นไหวต่อสิ่งใด
eng_definition: unable to be upset or excited; calm
example_sentence: Despite the chaos in the emergency room, the head nurse remained entirely imperturbable.

thai_meaning: ไม่สามารถทะลุผ่านได้, ไม่สะทกสะท้านต่อ...
eng_definition: not allowing fluid to pass through; unable to be affected by
example_sentence: He seemed impervious to all the criticism directed at him.

thai_meaning: หุนหันพลันแล่น, ทำโดยไม่คิด
eng_definition: acting or done quickly and without thought or care
example_sentence: Her impetuous decision to quit her job left her scrambling for income.

thai_meaning: ไม่สามารถทำให้สงบหรือพอใจได้, ดื้อรั้น
eng_definition: unable to be placated or pacified; relentless
example_sentence: The rebels proved to be an implacable enemy of the state.

thai_meaning: รบเร้า, อ้อนวอนอย่างต่อเนื่องจนน่ารำคาญ
eng_definition: harass (someone) persistently for or to do something
example_sentence: Street vendors would constantly importune tourists to buy their souvenirs.

thai_meaning: กล่าวหา, โจมตีความน่าเชื่อถือ
eng_definition: dispute the truth, validity, or honesty of (a statement or motive); call into question
example_sentence: The defense attorney tried to impugn the witness's credibility.

thai_meaning: การลอยนวลพ้นผิด, การได้รับการยกเว้นโทษ
eng_definition: exemption from punishment or freedom from the injurious consequences of an action
example_sentence: Corrupt officials acted with impunity because they controlled the justice system.

thai_meaning: เพิ่งเริ่มต้น, ยังไม่เป็นรูปเป็นร่าง
eng_definition: just begun and so not fully formed or developed; rudimentary
example_sentence: The startup's business plan was still inchoate and lacked crucial details.

thai_meaning: เฉียบแหลม, ชัดเจนและตรงประเด็น (ความคิดหรือคำพูด)
eng_definition: (of a person or mental process) intelligently analytical and clear-thinking
example_sentence: The critic's incisive review perfectly summarized the film's flaws.

thai_meaning: การบุกรุก, การรุกล้ำ (มักเกิดขึ้นฉับพลัน)
eng_definition: an invasion or attack, especially a sudden or brief one
example_sentence: The military built defensive walls to prevent further border incursions.

thai_meaning: ไม่รู้จักเหน็ดเหนื่อย, ทรหด
eng_definition: (of a person or their efforts) persisting tirelessly
example_sentence: She was an indefatigable campaigner for human rights.

thai_meaning: ยากไร้, ขัดสน
eng_definition: poor; needy
example_sentence: The clinic provides free medical care to indigent patients.

thai_meaning: เกียจคร้าน, ไม่ชอบทำงาน
eng_definition: wanting to avoid activity or exertion; lazy
example_sentence: The indolent teenager spent the entire summer playing video games.

thai_meaning: หลีกเลี่ยงไม่ได้, ต้านทานไม่ได้
eng_definition: unable to be resisted or avoided; inescapable
