example_sentence: Aging is an ineluctable process that everyone must face.

thai_meaning: ไม่หยุดยั้ง, ไม่ยอมผ่อนปรน
eng_definition: impossible to stop or prevent
example_sentence: The inexorable rise of artificial intelligence is transforming the job market.

thai_meaning: ไม่เหมาะสม, โชคร้าย
eng_definition: unfortunate; inappropriate
example_sentence: His infelicitous comment about the bride's dress caused an awkward silence.

thai_meaning: ซื่อตรง, ไร้เดียงสา, เปิดเผย
eng_definition: (of a person or action) innocent and unsuspecting
example_sentence: It was ingenuous of her to think that the salesman had her best interests at heart.

thai_meaning: เป็นปรปักษ์, เป็นอันตรายต่อ
eng_definition: tending to obstruct or harm; unfriendly, hostile
example_sentence: Policies that restrict free trade are often inimical to economic growth.

thai_meaning: ความอยุติธรรมอย่างร้ายแรง, ความชั่วร้าย
eng_definition: immoral or grossly unfair behavior
example_sentence: The documentary exposed the dark iniquity of the human trafficking network.

thai_meaning: ไม่มีอันตราย, ไม่ทำร้ายใคร
eng_definition: not harmful or offensive
example_sentence: It seemed like an innocuous question, but it sparked a heated argument.

thai_meaning: ไม่ถูกสุขลักษณะ, เสื่อมโทรม
eng_definition: (especially of a place) seedy, unwholesome, and badly kept
example_sentence: The refugees were forced to live in cramped and insalubrious conditions.

thai_meaning: ยากที่จะเข้าใจ, ลึกลับหยั่งไม่ถึง
eng_definition: impossible to understand or interpret
example_sentence: The CEO wore an inscrutable expression, leaving the board guessing about his intentions.

thai_meaning: จืดชืด, น่าเบื่อ, ไร้รสชาติ
eng_definition: lacking flavor; lacking vigor or interest
example_sentence: I found the artist's new collection to be rather insipid compared to his earlier work.

thai_meaning: ไม่แยแส, ไม่สนใจไยดี, ไร้กังวล
eng_definition: showing a casual lack of concern; indifferent
example_sentence: He wandered into the crucial meeting late, with an insouciant smile on his face.

thai_meaning: คับแคบ (ความคิด), โดดเดี่ยว
eng_definition: ignorant of or uninterested in cultures, ideas, or peoples outside one's own experience
example_sentence: Living in a small, insular community made it hard for her to adapt to the diverse city.

thai_meaning: จัดการยาก, ดื้อรั้น, (ปัญหา) แก้ไขยาก
eng_definition: hard to control or deal with; (of a person) stubborn
example_sentence: Poverty in the region remains an intractable problem despite decades of aid.

thai_meaning: ไม่ยอมประนีประนอม, หัวแข็ง
eng_definition: unwilling or refusing to change one's views or to agree about something
example_sentence: The union leaders remained intransigent in their demand for higher wages.

thai_meaning: กล้าหาญ, ไม่เกรงกลัวอันตราย
eng_definition: fearless; adventurous (often used for rhetorical or humorous effect)
example_sentence: Our intrepid reporter is broadcasting live from the center of the hurricane.

thai_meaning: ชินชาต่อสิ่งเลวร้าย, ชินกับความลำบาก
eng_definition: accustom (someone) to something, especially something unpleasant
example_sentence: Doctors working in the war zone quickly became inured to the sight of suffering.

thai_meaning: ผรุสวาท, คำด่าทอที่รุนแรง
eng_definition: insulting, abusive, or highly critical language
example_sentence: The debate devolved into a shouting match filled with political invective.

thai_meaning: ติดเป็นนิสัย, ฝังรากลึก
eng_definition: having a particular habit, activity, or interest that is long-established and unlikely to change
example_sentence: He is an inveterate liar who cannot be trusted with important secrets.

thai_meaning: น่ารังเกียจ, ซึ่งก่อให้เกิดความขุ่นเคือง
eng_definition: (of an action or situation) likely to arouse or incur resentment or anger in others
example_sentence: The manager was put in the invidious position of having to fire his best friend.

thai_meaning: ขี้โมโห, โกรธง่าย
eng_definition: having or showing a tendency to be easily angered
example_sentence: The irascible old professor would yell at students for merely whispering.

thai_meaning: ลังเล, ตัดสินใจไม่ได้
eng_definition: showing or feeling hesitancy; uncertain
example_sentence: She stood at the crossroads, completely irresolute about which path to take.

thai_meaning: ซึ่งเดินทางจากที่หนึ่งไปยังอีกที่หนึ่ง (โดยเฉพาะเพื่อทำงาน)
eng_definition: traveling from place to place
example_sentence: The itinerant workers move across the country following the harvest seasons.

thai_meaning: ไร้เดียงสาเกินไป, จืดชืดน่าเบื่อ
eng_definition: naive, simplistic, and superficial; (of ideas or writings) dry and uninteresting
example_sentence: His jejune response to the complex ethical dilemma showed his lack of experience.

thai_meaning: ความคลั่งชาติ, ความรักชาติแบบก้าวร้าว
eng_definition: extreme patriotism, especially in the form of aggressive or warlike foreign policy
example_sentence: The politician's speech was full of dangerous jingoism intended to drum up support for the war.

thai_meaning: ตลกขบขัน, ขี้เล่น
eng_definition: fond of or characterized by joking; humorous or playful
example_sentence: He was in a jocular mood, making everyone at the dinner table laugh.

thai_meaning: หมอบกราบ, ยอมจำนนหรือประจบสอพลออย่างมาก
eng_definition: act in an excessively subservient manner
example_sentence: The dictator demanded that all his ministers kowtow to his every whim.

thai_meaning: เจ้าน้ำตา, เศร้าโศก
eng_definition: tearful or given to weeping; inducing tears
example_sentence: The lachrymose drama had half the audience reaching for their tissues.

thai_meaning: พูดน้อย, ใช้คำสั้นๆ กะทัดรัด
eng_definition: (of a person, speech, or style of writing) using very few words
example_sentence: His laconic reply of 'Maybe' gave us no clue as to what he was thinking.

thai_meaning: ความอ่อนเพลีย, ความเกียจคร้านไร้เรี่ยวแรง
eng_definition: a state of physical or mental weariness; lack of energy
example_sentence: Overcome by the intense heat, she surrendered to a feeling of complete lassitude.

thai_meaning: แอบแฝง, ซ่อนเร้น (ยังไม่ปรากฏออกมา)
eng_definition: (of a quality or state) existing but not yet developed or manifest; hidden or concealed
example_sentence: The teacher helped the student discover his latent talent for music.

thai_meaning: เต็มไปด้วยคำสรรเสริญเยินยอ
eng_definition: (of speech or writing) expressing praise and commendation
example_sentence: The author received laudatory reviews from several prominent literary critics.

thai_meaning: เฉื่อยชา, ง่วงซึม
eng_definition: affected by lethargy; sluggish and apathetic
example_sentence: After eating the massive holiday dinner, everyone felt too lethargic to go for a walk.

thai_meaning: คันกั้นน้ำ
eng_definition: an embankment built to prevent the overflow of a river
example_sentence: The city flooded completely after the levee broke during the hurricane.

thai_meaning: ความไม่เอาจริงเอาจัง (ในสถานการณ์ที่ควรจริงจัง), การพูดติดตลก
eng_definition: humor or frivolity, especially the treatment of a serious matter with humor or in a manner lacking due respect
example_sentence: He attempted to introduce some levity into the tense courtroom.

thai_meaning: ผู้ทำตามอำเภอใจ, คนมักมากในกาม
eng_definition: a person, especially a man, who behaves without moral principles or a sense of responsibility
example_sentence: His reputation as a wealthy libertine made him scandalous in polite society.

thai_meaning: ใสแจ๋ว, ชัดเจนเข้าใจง่าย
eng_definition: (of a liquid) free of anything that darkens; completely clear
example_sentence: We could see the fish swimming in the limpid waters of the tropical lagoon.

thai_meaning: ยกย่องเทิดทูนราวกับคนดัง
eng_definition: give a lot of public attention and approval to (someone); treat as a celebrity
example_sentence: Modern culture tends to lionize athletes and actors more than scientists.

thai_meaning: ช่างพูดช่างเจรจา
eng_definition: tending to talk a great deal; talkative
example_sentence: The loquacious taxi driver told us his entire life story during the short ride.

thai_meaning: เรืองแสง, โปร่งแสง
eng_definition: glowing with or giving off light
example_sentence: The moon cast a lucent glow over the quiet, snow-covered landscape.

thai_meaning: ฉูดฉาดเกินไป, น่าสยดสยอง (เพื่อเรียกร้องความสนใจ)
eng_definition: very vivid in color, especially so as to create an unpleasantly harsh or unnatural effect; presented in vividly shocking terms
example_sentence: The tabloid newspapers published lurid details of the celebrity's divorce.

thai_meaning: อุบาย, การวางแผนลับๆ ที่มีเจตนาร้าย
eng_definition: a plot or scheme
example_sentence: The king was ultimately overthrown by the political machinations of his own advisors.

thai_meaning: กระแสน้ำวนที่รุนแรง, สภาวะที่ปั่นป่วนวุ่นวายสุดๆ
eng_definition: a powerful whirlpool in the sea or a river; a situation or state of confused movement or violent turmoil
example_sentence: Following the assassination, the entire country was plunged into a maelstrom of violence.

thai_meaning: ใจกว้าง, ให้อภัย (โดยเฉพาะต่อผู้ที่ด้อยกว่าหรือศัตรู)
eng_definition: very generous or forgiving, especially toward a rival or someone less powerful than oneself
example_sentence: In a magnanimous gesture, the winning team praised their defeated rivals.

thai_meaning: งุ่มง่าม, ไม่ชำนาญ, ขาดชั้นเชิง
eng_definition: ineffective or bungling; clumsy
example_sentence: His maladroit attempt at an apology only made the situation worse.

thai_meaning: คำสาปแช่ง
eng_definition: a magical word or phrase uttered with the intention of bringing about evil or destruction; a curse
example_sentence: The witch supposedly muttered a malediction against the villagers who banished her.

thai_meaning: การปฏิบัติหน้าที่โดยมิชอบ (โดยเฉพาะของเจ้าหน้าที่รัฐ)
eng_definition: wrongdoing, especially by a public official
example_sentence: The mayor was investigated for financial malfeasance during his term in office.

thai_meaning: แกล้งป่วยเพื่อหลบเลี่ยงงาน
eng_definition: exaggerate or feign illness in order to escape duty or work
example_sentence: The sergeant suspected the private was malingering when he claimed his back hurt.

thai_meaning: ดัดหรือตีให้เป็นแผ่นได้ง่าย, ว่านอนสอนง่าย
eng_definition: (of a metal or other material) able to be hammered or pressed permanently out of shape without breaking or cracking; easily influenced
example_sentence: Children's minds are highly malleable and absorb information rapidly.

thai_meaning: เกี่ยวกับทหารหรือการสงคราม
eng_definition: of or appropriate to war; warlike
example_sentence: The dictator declared martial law to quickly suppress the nationwide protests.

thai_meaning: ผู้ที่เข้มงวดเจ้าระเบียบมาก (โดยเฉพาะในกองทัพ)
eng_definition: a strict disciplinarian, especially in the armed forces
example_sentence: The new headmaster was a martinet who expected absolute obedience from the students.

thai_meaning: เศร้าโศกฟูมฟายเกินเหตุ (มักเกิดจากอาการเมา)
eng_definition: self-pityingly or tearfully sentimental, often through drunkenness
example_sentence: After his third glass of wine, he became maudlin and started crying about his ex-girlfriend.

thai_meaning: คนที่มีความคิดแหวกแนว, ผู้ไม่ยอมทำตามกฎระเบียบเดิมๆ
eng_definition: an unorthodox or independent-minded person
example_sentence: He was considered a maverick in the tech industry because he completely ignored standard business practices.

thai_meaning: ไพเราะหู, หวานชื่น (เสียง)
eng_definition: (of a voice or words) sweet or musical; pleasant to hear
example_sentence: The radio host had a mellifluous voice that put his listeners completely at ease.

thai_meaning: ชอบพูดโกหก, ไม่จริงใจ
eng_definition: not telling the truth; lying
example_sentence: The politician's mendacious claims were quickly debunked by fact-checkers.

thai_meaning: ขอทาน
eng_definition: a beggar
example_sentence: The streets of the medieval city were filled with mendicants asking for coins.

thai_meaning: อารมณ์แปรปรวนง่าย, เปลี่ยนแปลงรวดเร็ว
eng_definition: (of a person) subject to sudden or unpredictable changes of mood or mind
example_sentence: The artist's mercurial temperament made her very difficult to work with.

thai_meaning: สวยแต่รูปจูบไม่หอม, ดูดีแต่ไม่มีคุณค่าจริง
eng_definition: apparently attractive but having in reality no value or integrity
example_sentence: He was fooled by the meretricious promises of the get-rich-quick scheme.

thai_meaning: พิถีพิถัน, รอบคอบมาก
eng_definition: showing great attention to detail; very careful and precise
example_sentence: She kept meticulous records of all the company's financial transactions.

thai_meaning: ผู้ที่เกลียดชังมนุษย์
eng_definition: a person who dislikes humankind and avoids human society
example_sentence: As he grew older, he became a misanthrope who refused to leave his isolated cabin.

thai_meaning: ทำให้สงบ, บรรเทาความโกรธ
eng_definition: appease the anger or anxiety of (someone)
example_sentence: The airline offered free vouchers in an attempt to mollify the angry passengers.

thai_meaning: ใกล้ตาย, กำลังจะล่มสลาย
eng_definition: (of a person) at the point of death; (of a thing) in terminal decline
example_sentence: The new CEO was hired to revive the company's moribund sales.

thai_meaning: บึ้งตึง, ซึมเศร้าอารมณ์เสีย
eng_definition: sullen and ill-tempered
example_sentence: After losing the match, he sat in a morose silence for the rest of the evening.

thai_meaning: หลากหลาย, มีหลายรูปแบบ
eng_definition: many and of various types
example_sentence: The job required a candidate capable of handling multifarious tasks simultaneously.

thai_meaning: ธรรมดา, ทางโลก, น่าเบื่อ
eng_definition: lacking interest or excitement; dull; of this earthly world rather than a heavenly or spiritual one
example_sentence: He found the mundane tasks of office work completely unfulfilling.

thai_meaning: ใจกว้างมาก, เอื้อเฟื้อเผื่อแผ่สุดๆ
eng_definition: (of a gift or sum of money) larger or more generous than is usual or necessary
example_sentence: The university was saved from bankruptcy by a munificent donation from an anonymous alumnus.

thai_meaning: สายตาสั้น (ทั้งทางกายภาพและวิสัยทัศน์)
eng_definition: nearsighted; lacking imagination, foresight, or intellectual insight
example_sentence: The government's myopic approach to environmental issues will cost us dearly in the future.

thai_meaning: เพิ่งเกิด, เริ่มก่อตัว
eng_definition: (especially of a process or organization) just coming into existence and beginning to display signs of future potential
example_sentence: The country's nascent democracy is still very fragile and requires international support.

thai_meaning: คลุมเครือ, พร่ามัว, ไม่ชัดเจน
eng_definition: in the form of a cloud or haze; hazy; (of a concept or idea) unclear, vague, or ill-defined
example_sentence: The politician gave a nebulous answer that completely avoided the journalist's question.

thai_meaning: ชั่วร้าย, เลวทราม (มักใช้กับอาชญากรรม)
eng_definition: (typically of an action or activity) wicked or criminal
example_sentence: The hackers used nefarious means to steal millions of dollars from ordinary citizens.

thai_meaning: ผู้เริ่มต้น, มือใหม่
eng_definition: a person who is new to a subject, skill, or belief
example_sentence: As a neophyte in the kitchen, she struggled to boil water without causing a disaster.

thai_meaning: แต่เพียงในนาม, (ราคา) เล็กน้อยมากจนแทบไม่มีความหมาย
eng_definition: (of a role or status) existing in name only; (of a price or amount of money) very small; far below the real value or cost
example_sentence: The king is merely the nominal head of state, while the prime minister holds the real power.

thai_meaning: ทำให้สับสน, ทำให้คลุมเครือ
eng_definition: render obscure, unclear, or unintelligible
example_sentence: The politician used technical jargon to obfuscate the real issues.

thai_meaning: ดื้อรั้น, เอะอะโวยวาย, ควบคุมยาก
eng_definition: noisy and difficult to control
example_sentence: The teacher struggled to manage the class of obstreperous teenagers.

thai_meaning: ที่ชอบสอดรู้สอดเห็น, เด่นชัดจนน่ารำคาญ
eng_definition: noticeable or prominent in an unwelcome or intrusive way
example_sentence: The new skyscraper is an obtrusive addition to the historic skyline.

thai_meaning: ขจัด(ปัญหา), ป้องกันล่วงหน้า
eng_definition: remove (a need or difficulty); avoid or prevent
example_sentence: The new medical treatment obviates the need for invasive surgery.

thai_meaning: ปิดกั้น, อุดตัน
eng_definition: stop, close up, or obstruct (an opening, orifice, or passage)
example_sentence: A blood clot can occlude a major artery and cause a heart attack.

thai_meaning: น่ารังเกียจมาก, น่าสะอิดสะเอียน
eng_definition: extremely unpleasant; repulsive
example_sentence: Cleaning the public restrooms was the most odious task of the day.

thai_meaning: เจ้ากี้เจ้าการ, ชอบออกคำสั่งในเรื่องไม่เป็นเรื่อง
eng_definition: assertive of authority in an annoyingly domineering way, especially with regard to petty or trivial matters
example_sentence: The officious security guard demanded to see everyone's ID twice.

thai_meaning: เป็นภาระหนัก, ยากลำบาก
eng_definition: (of a task, duty, or responsibility) involving an amount of effort and difficulty that is oppressively burdensome
example_sentence: He found the onerous duties of the new management position overwhelming.

thai_meaning: ความอัปยศอดสู, การถูกสังคมประณาม
eng_definition: harsh criticism or censure; the public disgrace arising from someone's shameful conduct
example_sentence: The corrupt politician was forced to resign amidst public opprobrium.

thai_meaning: ที่แสดงออกให้เห็นภายนอก (แต่มักไม่เป็นความจริง), ที่เสแสร้ง
eng_definition: stated or appearing to be true, but not necessarily so
example_sentence: The ostensible reason for his visit was to see his aunt, but he really came to borrow money.

thai_meaning: โอ้อวด, ขี้โอ่, หรูหราเกินความจำเป็น
eng_definition: characterized by vulgar or pretentious display; designed to impress or attract notice
example_sentence: She wore an ostentatious diamond ring that blinded everyone in the room.

thai_meaning: หยิ่งยโสเกินไป, มั่นใจในตัวเองมากเกินไป
eng_definition: showing excessive confidence or pride
example_sentence: His overweening ambition led him to betray his closest friends.

thai_meaning: บรรเทา (อาการเจ็บปวดหรือปัญหา) โดยไม่รักษาที่ต้นเหตุ
eng_definition: make (a disease or its symptoms) less severe or unpleasant without removing the cause
example_sentence: The medication is only intended to palliate the symptoms, not cure the disease.

thai_meaning: ยารักษาได้ทุกโรค, วิธีแก้ปัญหาได้ทุกอย่าง
eng_definition: a solution or remedy for all difficulties or diseases
example_sentence: Technology is not a panacea for all the world's complex social issues.

thai_meaning: คำสรรเสริญเยินยอ, บทความยกย่อง
eng_definition: a public speech or published text in praise of someone or something
example_sentence: The mayor delivered a moving panegyric at the retiring police chief's farewell dinner.

thai_meaning: กระบวนทัศน์, แบบจำลอง, ตัวอย่างที่ชัดเจน
eng_definition: a typical example or pattern of something; a model
example_sentence: The discovery of DNA created a new paradigm in biological science.

thai_meaning: บุคคลที่เป็นแบบอย่างของความดีเลิศ
eng_definition: a person or thing regarded as a perfect example of a particular quality
example_sentence: He was considered a paragon of virtue in his community.

thai_meaning: ผู้ที่ถูกสังคมรังเกียจและขับไล่
eng_definition: an outcast
example_sentence: After the embezzlement scandal, the former executive became a social pariah.

thai_meaning: ขี้เหนียว, ตระหนี่ถี่เหนียว
eng_definition: unwilling to spend money or use resources; stingy or frugal
example_sentence: The parsimonious old man refused to turn on the heating even in mid-winter.

thai_meaning: ความขาดแคลน, การมีอยู่น้อยมาก
eng_definition: the presence of something only in small or insufficient quantities or amounts; scarcity
example_sentence: There is a paucity of reliable data regarding the long-term effects of this diet.

thai_meaning: ความผิดเล็กๆ น้อยๆ
eng_definition: a small, relatively unimportant offense or sin
example_sentence: The politician's sexual peccadilloes were heavily scrutinized by the media.

thai_meaning: อวดรู้, เจ้าระเบียบเรื่องหยุมหยิม
eng_definition: excessively concerned with minor details and rules or with displaying academic learning
example_sentence: His pedantic lecture style bored the students to tears.

thai_meaning: ที่ดูถูกเหยียดหยาม, ในแง่ลบ
eng_definition: expressing contempt or disapproval
example_sentence: The term has increasingly been used in a pejorative sense to mock young people.

thai_meaning: ใสแจ๋ว, ชัดเจนเข้าใจง่ายสุดๆ
eng_definition: translucently clear; easily understood
example_sentence: She gave a pellucid explanation of the complex macroeconomic theory.

thai_meaning: ความชื่นชอบเป็นพิเศษ, รสนิยม
eng_definition: a strong or habitual liking for something or tendency to do something
example_sentence: He has a penchant for adopting stray animals he finds on the street.

thai_meaning: ความยากจนข้นแค้น
eng_definition: extreme poverty; destitution
example_sentence: Many authors died in penury before their masterpieces became famous.

thai_meaning: ทรยศ, ไว้ใจไม่ได้
eng_definition: deceitful and untrustworthy
example_sentence: The perfidious ally switched sides halfway through the brutal war.

thai_meaning: ทำพอเป็นพิธี, ทำส่งๆ ไป
eng_definition: (of an action or gesture) carried out with a minimum of effort or reflection
example_sentence: He gave a perfunctory nod but didn't even look up from his phone.

thai_meaning: ที่เดินทางรอนแรมไปเรื่อยๆ
eng_definition: traveling from place to place, in particular working or based in various places for relatively short periods
example_sentence: The peripatetic musician lived out of his van for almost a decade.

thai_meaning: เป็นอันตรายอย่างยิ่ง (มักเป็นไปอย่างเงียบๆ ค่อยเป็นค่อยไป)
eng_definition: having a harmful effect, especially in a gradual or subtle way
example_sentence: The pernicious effects of the propaganda took years to undo.

thai_meaning: มีสายตาแหลมคม, เข้าใจทะลุปรุโปร่ง
eng_definition: having a ready insight into and understanding of things
example_sentence: The perspicacious detective quickly saw through the suspect's alibi.

thai_meaning: ยืนหยัด, ดื้อดึง, ไม่ยอมลดละ
eng_definition: holding firmly to an opinion or a course of action
example_sentence: Like a pertinacious bulldog, the lawyer refused to drop the difficult case.

thai_meaning: เจ้าอารมณ์, หงุดหงิดง่ายแบบเด็กๆ
eng_definition: (of a person or their manner) childishly sulky or bad-tempered
example_sentence: He behaved like a petulant child when he realized he wasn't going to get his way.

thai_meaning: เฉื่อยชา, ไม่ค่อยแสดงอารมณ์, ใจเย็น
eng_definition: (of a person) having an unemotional and stolidly calm disposition
example_sentence: Her phlegmatic nature made her an excellent crisis negotiator.

thai_meaning: มีรสจัดจ้าน, เผ็ดร้อนน่าสนใจ
eng_definition: having a pleasantly sharp taste or appetizing flavor; pleasantly stimulating or exciting to the mind
example_sentence: The chef added a piquant sauce that completely transformed the dish.

thai_meaning: ได้ใจความ, สั้นกระชับแต่มีความหมายลึกซึ้ง
eng_definition: (of language or style) concise and forcefully expressive
example_sentence: The book is full of pithy observations about human nature.

thai_meaning: คำพูดซ้ำซากจำเจ, คำสอนที่ใช้จนเฝือ
eng_definition: a remark or statement, especially one with a moral content, that has been used too often to be interesting or thoughtful
example_sentence: The CEO offered nothing but empty platitudes instead of a real plan to save the company.

thai_meaning: จำนวนมากมายก่ายกอง, ภาวะที่มีมากเกินไป
eng_definition: a large or excessive amount of (something)
example_sentence: The internet offers a plethora of information, but not all of it is accurate.

thai_meaning: บทความหรือคำพูดที่โจมตีโต้แย้งอย่างรุนแรง
eng_definition: a strong verbal or written attack on someone or something
example_sentence: Her latest book is a fierce polemic against the modern education system.

thai_meaning: เชื่องช้าและอุ้ยอ้าย, น่าเบื่อหน่าย (เพราะหนักหรือยาวเกินไป)
eng_definition: slow and clumsy because of great weight; dull, laborious, or excessively solemn
example_sentence: The elephant moved with ponderous steps through the dense jungle.

thai_meaning: เป็นลางร้าย, ซึ่งแสดงความสำคัญเกินจริง
eng_definition: of or like a portent (sign or warning of a momentous or calamitous event); done in a pompously or overly solemn manner so as to impress
example_sentence: The dark, portentous clouds gathered just before the massive storm hit.

thai_meaning: เร่งให้เกิดขึ้นเร็วกว่ากำหนด, ผลีผลาม
eng_definition: cause (an event or situation, typically one that is bad or undesirable) to happen suddenly, unexpectedly, or prematurely
example_sentence: The assassination of the archduke helped precipitate the First World War.

thai_meaning: ขัดขวาง, ทำให้เป็นไปไม่ได้
eng_definition: prevent from happening; make impossible
example_sentence: His criminal record will preclude him from ever holding public office.

thai_meaning: แก่แดด, มีพัฒนาการเร็วกว่าวัย
eng_definition: (of a child) having developed certain abilities or proclivities at an earlier age than usual
example_sentence: The precocious child was already reading complex literature at age five.

thai_meaning: ความลำเอียง, ความชอบพอเป็นพิเศษ
eng_definition: a preference or special liking for something; a bias in favor of something
example_sentence: She has a predilection for spicy food and adds chili to almost everything.

thai_meaning: การรู้อนาคต, ญาณหยั่งรู้
eng_definition: the fact of knowing something before it takes place; foreknowledge
example_sentence: With remarkable prescience, he sold all his stocks just days before the market crashed.

thai_meaning: ทะลึ่ง, ข้ามหน้าข้ามตา, ถือวิสาสะ
eng_definition: (of a person or their behavior) failing to observe the limits of what is permitted or appropriate
example_sentence: It was highly presumptuous of him to assume I would pay for his expensive dinner.

thai_meaning: พูดบ่ายเบี่ยง, พูดอ้อมค้อมเพื่อเลี่ยงความจริง
eng_definition: speak or act in an evasive way
example_sentence: When asked about the missing funds, the manager began to prevaricate.

thai_meaning: ความซื่อสัตย์สุจริต
eng_definition: the quality of having strong moral principles; honesty and decency
example_sentence: The judge was a woman of unimpeachable probity who refused to take bribes.

thai_meaning: ความโน้มเอียง, นิสัยที่ชอบทำ (มักเป็นเรื่องไม่ดี)
eng_definition: a tendency to choose or do something regularly; an inclination or predisposition toward a particular thing
example_sentence: His proclivity for gambling eventually led him to bankruptcy.

thai_meaning: ฟุ่มเฟือย, สุรุ่ยสุร่าย
eng_definition: spending money or resources freely and recklessly; wastefully extravagant
example_sentence: The prodigal son squandered his entire inheritance within a few years.

thai_meaning: ใหญ่โตมโหฬาร, มหัศจรรย์มาก
eng_definition: remarkably or impressively great in extent, size, or degree
example_sentence: The team consumed a prodigious amount of pizza after winning the championship.

thai_meaning: เสเพล, ผลาญเงินทิ้งอย่างไร้ความรับผิดชอบ
eng_definition: recklessly extravagant or wasteful in the use of resources
example_sentence: The government was heavily criticized for its profligate spending on unnecessary infrastructure.

thai_meaning: มากมายก่ายกอง, ล้นหลาม
eng_definition: (especially of something offered or discharged) exuberantly plentiful; abundant
example_sentence: He broke out into profuse sweating as soon as the police entered the room.

thai_meaning: เพิ่มจำนวนอย่างรวดเร็ว, แพร่พันธุ์
eng_definition: increase rapidly in numbers; multiply
example_sentence: Fast-food restaurants have continued to proliferate across the globe.

thai_meaning: ผลิตผลงานออกมามากมาย, อุดมสมบูรณ์
eng_definition: (of a plant, animal, or person) producing much fruit or foliage or many offspring; present in large numbers or quantities
example_sentence: Stephen King is a highly prolific author who publishes multiple books every year.

thai_meaning: ความโน้มเอียงตามธรรมชาติ, ความชอบ
eng_definition: an inclination or natural tendency to behave in a particular way
example_sentence: He has a propensity to interrupt people when they are speaking.

thai_meaning: ทำให้พอใจ, เอาใจ (เพื่อลดความโกรธ)
eng_definition: win or regain the favor of (a god, spirit, or person) by doing something that pleases them
example_sentence: Ancient civilizations offered animal sacrifices to propitiate the angry gods.

thai_meaning: ความเหมาะสม, ความถูกต้องตามมารยาท
eng_definition: the state or quality of conforming to conventionally accepted standards of behavior or morals
example_sentence: He always behaves with the utmost propriety, no matter the situation.

thai_meaning: ธรรมดาเกินไป, จืดชืดไร้จินตนาการ
eng_definition: having the style or diction of prose; lacking poetic beauty; commonplace; unromantic
example_sentence: The truth behind the mystery turned out to be quite prosaic and boring.

thai_meaning: สั่งห้าม, ประกาศว่าผิดกฎหมาย
eng_definition: forbid, especially by law; denounce or condemn
example_sentence: The military regime decided to proscribe all forms of public protest.

thai_meaning: เปลี่ยนแปลงรูปร่างได้ง่าย, มีความสามารถหลากหลาย
eng_definition: tending or able to change frequently or easily; able to do many different things
example_sentence: The protean actor could seamlessly shift from comedy to deep tragedy.

thai_meaning: ทำตัวเป็นเด็ก, ไร้สาระ
eng_definition: childishly silly and trivial
example_sentence: The debate was ruined by the politicians' puerile insults toward each other.

thai_meaning: พิถีพิถันเรื่องกฎระเบียบและมารยาทอย่างมาก
eng_definition: showing great attention to detail or correct behavior
example_sentence: He was punctilious in providing every receipt for his business trip expenses.

thai_meaning: ผู้เชี่ยวชาญ, นักวิจารณ์การเมือง
eng_definition: an expert in a particular subject or field who is frequently called on to give opinions about it to the public
example_sentence: Political pundits predicted the election outcome entirely wrong.

thai_meaning: ขี้ขลาดตาขาว, ไม่มีความกล้าหาญ
eng_definition: showing a lack of courage or determination; timid
example_sentence: The pusillanimous leader refused to take a stand on the controversial issue.

thai_meaning: ปลักโคลน, สถานการณ์ที่กลืนไม่เข้าคายไม่ออก
eng_definition: a soft boggy area of land that gives way underfoot; an awkward, complex, or hazardous situation
example_sentence: The military intervention quickly turned into a political quagmire with no easy exit.

thai_meaning: ความรู้สึกตะขิดตะขวงใจ, ความไม่สบายใจ (เรื่องศีลธรรม)
eng_definition: an uneasy feeling of doubt, worry, or fear, especially about one's own conduct; a misgiving
example_sentence: He had no qualms about lying to get the promotion.

thai_meaning: ขี้บ่น, ชอบตัดพ้อต่อว่า
eng_definition: complaining in a petulant or whining manner
example_sentence: The querulous passenger complained about everything during the entire flight.

thai_meaning: ความสงบนิ่ง, ภาวะพักตัว
eng_definition: a state or period of inactivity or dormancy
example_sentence: The volcano has entered a period of long-term quiescence.

thai_meaning: เกิดขึ้นทุกวัน, เป็นกิจวัตรธรรมดา
eng_definition: of or occurring every day; daily; ordinary or everyday, especially when mundane
example_sentence: Washing dishes is just another one of life's quotidian chores.

thai_meaning: แตกกิ่งก้านสาขา, ทำให้เกิดผลกระทบสืบเนื่องซับซ้อน
eng_definition: form branches or offshoots; spread or branch out
example_sentence: The new legislation will ramify throughout the entire financial sector.

thai_meaning: เต็มไปด้วยความเคียดแค้น, อาฆาตพยาบาท
eng_definition: characterized by bitterness or resentment
example_sentence: The divorce became increasingly rancorous as they fought over the assets.

thai_meaning: ละโมบ, โลภมาก (มักใช้วิธีรุนแรง)
eng_definition: aggressively greedy or grasping
example_sentence: The rapacious corporation bought up and destroyed all its smaller competitors.

thai_meaning: เบาบาง (อากาศ), สูงส่งและเข้าใจยาก (สังคมหรือแนวคิด)
eng_definition: (of air, especially that at high altitudes) of lower pressure than usual; thin; distant from the lives and concerns of ordinary people
example_sentence: She struggled to fit into the rarefied atmosphere of the elite academic institution.

thai_meaning: ดื้อรั้น, ไม่ยอมเชื่อฟังคำสั่ง
eng_definition: having an obstinately uncooperative attitude toward authority or discipline
example_sentence: A class of recalcitrant fifteen-year-olds can test the patience of any teacher.

thai_meaning: ถอนคำพูด, กลับคำให้การ
eng_definition: say that one no longer holds an opinion or belief, especially one considered heretical
example_sentence: Under immense pressure from the church, Galileo was forced to recant his scientific theories.

thai_meaning: ลึกซึ้งเข้าใจยาก, ลี้ลับ
eng_definition: (of a subject or knowledge) little known; abstruse
example_sentence: The book discusses recondite philosophical concepts that only a few scholars understand.

thai_meaning: น่าเกรงขาม, น่ายำเกรง (มักใช้ในแง่บวก)
eng_definition: (of a person) formidable, especially as an opponent
example_sentence: He was a redoubtable debater who had never lost an argument.

thai_meaning: ดื้อดึง, รักษายาก (โรค)
eng_definition: stubborn or unmanageable; resistant to a process or stimulus
example_sentence: The patient suffered from a refractory form of cancer that did not respond to chemotherapy.

thai_meaning: เปล่งประกายเจิดจ้า
eng_definition: shining brightly
example_sentence: The bride looked absolutely stunning in her refulgent silk gown.

thai_meaning: ลดตำแหน่ง, ขับไสให้ไปอยู่ในที่ที่แย่กว่า
eng_definition: consign or dismiss to an inferior rank or position
example_sentence: After the massive failure, he was relegated to a minor administrative role.

thai_meaning: ประท้วง, โต้แย้งอย่างมีเหตุผล
eng_definition: make a forcefully reproachful protest
example_sentence: He went to the manager to remonstrate against the unfair working conditions.

thai_meaning: ผิดสัญญา, ตระบัดสัตย์
eng_definition: go back on a promise, undertaking, or contract
synonym: default, break one's word, back out
example_sentence: The government was accused of trying to renege on its campaign promises.

thai_meaning: บ่นพึมพำ, เศร้าโศกเสียใจ
eng_definition: feel or express discontent; fret
example_sentence: There is no use in repining over mistakes that cannot be undone.

thai_meaning: คนเสเพล, คนไร้ศีลธรรม
eng_definition: an unprincipled person (often used humorously or affectionately)
example_sentence: The old reprobate spent most of his evenings drinking at the local tavern.

thai_meaning: ปฏิเสธ, ไม่ยอมรับ (ความจริง หรือ ข้อกล่าวหา)
eng_definition: refuse to accept or be associated with; deny the truth or validity of
example_sentence: The company's board of directors voted to repudiate the hostile takeover bid.

thai_meaning: เพิกถอน, ยกเลิก (กฎหมาย, สัญญา)
eng_definition: revoke, cancel, or repeal (a law, order, or agreement)
example_sentence: The court decided to rescind the earlier ruling due to new evidence.

thai_meaning: เก็บตัว, สงวนคำพูด, ไม่ชอบแสดงความรู้สึก
eng_definition: not revealing one's thoughts or feelings readily
example_sentence: She was deeply reticent about her painful childhood experiences.

thai_meaning: ที่แสดงความเคารพอย่างสูง
eng_definition: feeling or showing deep and solemn respect
example_sentence: A reverent silence fell over the crowd as the memorial service began.

thai_meaning: น่าหัวเราะเยาะ, ไร้สาระจนน่าขำ
eng_definition: such as to provoke laughter
example_sentence: His excuse for being late was so risible that even the teacher laughed.

thai_meaning: ครุ่นคิด, ไตร่ตรองอย่างลึกซึ้ง
eng_definition: think deeply about something
example_sentence: He went for a long walk alone to ruminate on the complex ethical problem.

thai_meaning: เฉียบแหลม, มีวิจารณญาณที่ดี, ฉลาดหลักแหลม
eng_definition: having or showing keen mental discernment and good judgment
example_sentence: The sagacious old mentor offered guidance that saved the business from ruin.

thai_meaning: ดีต่อสุขภาพ, สภาพแวดล้อมที่น่าอยู่
eng_definition: health-giving; healthy; (of a place) pleasant; not run-down
example_sentence: They moved to the countryside seeking a more salubrious environment.

thai_meaning: เป็นประโยชน์, เป็นผลดี (แม้ว่ามักจะมาจากประสบการณ์ที่ไม่พึงประสงค์)
eng_definition: (especially with reference to something unwelcome or unpleasant) producing good effects; beneficial
example_sentence: The severe economic recession had a salutary effect by forcing companies to become more efficient.

thai_meaning: ทำตัวเป็นคนดีมีศีลธรรมเหนือผู้อื่น (หน้าไหว้หลังหลอก)
eng_definition: making a show of being morally superior to other people
example_sentence: Everyone resented his sanctimonious lectures about how they should live their lives.

thai_meaning: ร่าเริง, มองโลกในแง่ดี (แม้ในสถานการณ์ที่ย่ำแย่)
eng_definition: optimistic or positive, especially in an apparently bad or difficult situation
example_sentence: Despite the team's continuous losing streak, the coach remained remarkably sanguine.

thai_meaning: เงียบขรึมและเศร้าหมอง, หน้าตาบึ้งตึง
eng_definition: (of a person or their manner) slow and gloomy
example_sentence: The stranger's saturnine face made the children afraid to approach him.

thai_meaning: การแตกแยก, การแบ่งฝักแบ่งฝ่าย (โดยเฉพาะในศาสนาหรือการเมือง)
eng_definition: a split or division between strongly opposed sections or parties, caused by differences in opinion or belief
example_sentence: A bitter theological dispute caused a permanent schism within the church.

thai_meaning: เศษเสี้ยวเล็กๆ, ปริมาณน้อยนิด
eng_definition: a tiny trace or spark of a specified quality or feeling
example_sentence: There is not a scintilla of evidence to support these outrageous accusations.

thai_meaning: สละราชสมบัติ, ลาออกจากตำแหน่งหน้าที่
eng_definition: to renounce one's throne or fail to fulfill a responsibility or duty
example_sentence: The aging monarch decided to abdicate the throne in favor of his eldest son.

thai_meaning: รางวัล, เกียรติยศ, การยกย่อง
eng_definition: an award or privilege granted as a special honor or as an acknowledgment of merit
example_sentence: The scientist received the highest accolade for his groundbreaking discovery in physics.

thai_meaning: ยอมรับ, ยอมทำตามโดยไม่เต็มใจแต่ไม่คัดค้าน
eng_definition: to accept something reluctantly but without protest
example_sentence: He will simply acquiesce to their demands to avoid a prolonged argument.

thai_meaning: รุนแรง, ดุเดือด (คำพูดหรือการโต้เถียง)
eng_definition: typically of speech or a debate, angry and bitter
example_sentence: The divorce proceedings became highly acrimonious over the division of assets.

thai_meaning: ความมีไหวพริบ, ความเฉียบแหลม
eng_definition: the ability to make good judgments and quick decisions, typically in a particular domain
example_sentence: Her business acumen allowed her to turn a failing company into a global empire.

thai_meaning: ความกระตือรือร้น, ความรวดเร็วและเต็มใจ
eng_definition: brisk and cheerful readiness
example_sentence: She accepted the job offer with alacrity and started working the very next day.

thai_meaning: รวมตัวกัน, ผสมผสานเข้าด้วยกัน
eng_definition: to combine or unite to form one organization or structure
example_sentence: The two competing tech companies decided to amalgamate into a single powerful corporation.

thai_meaning: ทำให้ดีขึ้น, ปรับปรุง (สถานการณ์ที่แย่)
eng_definition: to make something bad or unsatisfactory better
example_sentence: Steps have been taken to ameliorate the living conditions in the crowded refugee camp.

thai_meaning: สิ่งที่ไม่เข้ากับยุคสมัย, การผิดยุคผิดสมัย
eng_definition: a thing belonging or appropriate to a period other than that in which it exists
example_sentence: Using a typewriter in a modern software development office feels like a complete anachronism.

thai_meaning: ความเกลียดชัง, ความเป็นปรปักษ์
eng_definition: strong hostility or hatred
example_sentence: There is still a great deal of animosity between the two rival political parties.

thai_meaning: ความไร้อารมณ์, ความเมินเฉยไม่ใส่ใจ
eng_definition: lack of interest, enthusiasm, or concern
example_sentence: Voter apathy is a serious problem that threatens the foundation of our democracy.

thai_meaning: ตามอำเภอใจ, ไม่มีเหตุผลมารองรับชัดเจน
eng_definition: based on random choice or personal whim, rather than any reason or system
example_sentence: The manager's decision to fire him seemed completely arbitrary and unfair.

thai_meaning: ลึกลับ, เข้าใจยาก (รู้เฉพาะกลุ่มคนเล็กๆ)
eng_definition: understood by few; mysterious or secret
example_sentence: He spent years studying arcane mathematical theories that few people can comprehend.

thai_meaning: สามารถพูดหรือถ่ายทอดความคิดได้อย่างชัดเจน
eng_definition: having or showing the ability to speak fluently and coherently
example_sentence: She is an articulate speaker who can easily captivate a large audience.

thai_meaning: สืบให้แน่ใจ, ค้นหาความจริง
eng_definition: to find something out for certain; make sure of
example_sentence: The detectives are trying to ascertain the exact sequence of events leading up to the crime.

thai_meaning: ที่บำเพ็ญตบะ, ถือศีล, งดเว้นจากความสุขทางโลก
eng_definition: characterized by or suggesting the practice of severe self-discipline and abstention from all forms of indulgence
example_sentence: The monk lived an ascetic life in a small cave high in the mountains.

thai_meaning: ดูดซึม, ปรับตัวเข้ากับวัฒนธรรมใหม่, ทำความเข้าใจอย่างถ่องแท้
eng_definition: to take in information, ideas, or culture and understand fully
example_sentence: It takes time for immigrants to assimilate into a new society and learn the customs.

thai_meaning: บรรเทา (ความรู้สึกที่ไม่ดี), ทำให้สงบลง
eng_definition: make an unpleasant feeling less intense
example_sentence: He tried to assuage his guilt by donating a large sum of money to charity.

thai_meaning: ฉลาดแกมโกง, มีไหวพริบดีเลิศ
eng_definition: having or showing an ability to accurately assess situations or people and turn this to one's advantage
example_sentence: An astute investor can make a profit even during a severe economic downturn.

thai_meaning: กล้าบ้าบิ่น, ไม่เกรงกลัวอันตราย, บังอาจ
eng_definition: showing a willingness to take surprisingly bold risks
example_sentence: His audacious plan to climb Mount Everest in winter shocked the climbing community.

thai_meaning: เพิ่มพูน, ทำให้มากขึ้น
eng_definition: to make something greater by adding to it; increase
example_sentence: She took on a second job to augment her income and save for a new car.

thai_meaning: เคร่งขรึม, เรียบง่ายมาก, ไม่หรูหรา
eng_definition: severe or strict in manner, attitude, or appearance; having no comforts or luxuries
example_sentence: The hospital room was austere, containing only a bed and a single wooden chair.

thai_meaning: ที่ปกครองตนเอง, เป็นอิสระ
eng_definition: having the freedom to govern itself or control its own affairs
example_sentence: The region demanded to be recognized as an autonomous state with its own laws.

thai_meaning: สัจพจน์, ความจริงที่เห็นได้ชัดโดยไม่ต้องพิสูจน์
eng_definition: a statement or proposition that is regarded as being established, accepted, or self-evidently true
example_sentence: It is a widely held axiom that you should never spend more than you earn.

thai_meaning: สนับสนุน, ค้ำจุน, เสริมกำลัง
eng_definition: to support or strengthen; prop up
example_sentence: The government introduced new policies to bolster the failing economy.

thai_meaning: ความสั้นกระชับ (ของเวลาหรือคำพูด)
eng_definition: concise and exact use of words in writing or speech; shortness of time
example_sentence: The brevity of his speech was appreciated by an audience tired of long lectures.

thai_meaning: หว่านล้อม, ประจบสอพลอเพื่อให้ทำตาม
eng_definition: to persuade someone to do something by sustained coaxing or flattery
example_sentence: He managed to cajole his little sister into doing his chores for him.

thai_meaning: ใจดำ, โหดร้าย, ไม่รู้สึกรู้สาต่อความเจ็บปวดของผู้อื่น
eng_definition: showing or having an insensitive and cruel disregard for others
example_sentence: His callous comment about her recent loss shocked everyone in the room.

thai_meaning: ตรงไปตรงมา, เปิดเผย, จริงใจ
eng_definition: truthful and straightforward; frank
example_sentence: I would appreciate your candid opinion on my new business proposal.

thai_meaning: ยอมจำนน, ยอมแพ้
eng_definition: cease to resist an opponent or an unwelcome demand; surrender
example_sentence: After weeks of intense pressure, the rebel forces finally decided to capitulate.

thai_meaning: ตำหนิอย่างรุนแรง (โดยเฉพาะอย่างเป็นทางการ)
eng_definition: express severe disapproval of someone or something, typically in a formal statement
example_sentence: The senator faced a vote of censure for his inappropriate conduct during the meeting.

thai_meaning: นักต้มตุ๋น, ผู้แอบอ้างว่ามีความรู้หรือทักษะ
eng_definition: a person falsely claiming to have a special knowledge or skill; a fraud
example_sentence: The man selling the miracle cure turned out to be a charlatan.

thai_meaning: เรื้อรัง, เกิดขึ้นอย่างต่อเนื่องยาวนาน
eng_definition: persisting for a long time or constantly recurring (often relating to an illness)
example_sentence: She has been suffering from chronic back pain for over a decade.

thai_meaning: จำกัดขอบเขต, ตีวง
eng_definition: to restrict something within limits
example_sentence: The new laws severely circumscribe the power of the local government.

thai_meaning: รอบคอบ, ระมัดระวัง (พิจารณาทุกแง่มุมก่อนทำ)
eng_definition: wary and unwilling to take risks
example_sentence: Given the delicate nature of the negotiations, the diplomats were highly circumspect.

thai_meaning: ที่ทำอย่างลับๆ, ปิดบังซ่อนเร้น
eng_definition: kept secret or done secretively, especially because illicit
example_sentence: The spies held a clandestine meeting in an abandoned warehouse.

thai_meaning: มีเมตตาปรานี, (อากาศ) อบอุ่นและสบาย
eng_definition: merciful; (of weather) mild
example_sentence: The judge was clement, handing down a lighter sentence than expected.

thai_meaning: หลอมรวมเป็นหนึ่งเดียว, รวมตัวกัน
eng_definition: come together and form one mass or whole
example_sentence: Various environmental groups have coalesced to form a powerful new political party.

thai_meaning: บังคับ, ขู่เข็ญให้ทำตาม
eng_definition: persuade an unwilling person to do something by using force or threats
example_sentence: The suspects claimed they were coerced into signing false confessions.

thai_meaning: ที่ตระหนักรู้, ที่รับทราบ
eng_definition: having knowledge or being aware of
example_sentence: We must be cognizant of the political implications of our actions.

thai_meaning: การสมรู้ร่วมคิด (ในทางทุจริต), การฮั้วกัน
eng_definition: secret or illegal cooperation or conspiracy, especially in order to cheat or deceive others
example_sentence: Several major companies were accused of collusion to keep prices artificially high.

thai_meaning: พึงพอใจในตัวเองจนเกินไป (จนประมาทหรือไม่พัฒนา)
eng_definition: showing smug or uncritical satisfaction with oneself or one's achievements
example_sentence: We cannot afford to become complacent despite our recent market success.

thai_meaning: ที่เกิดขึ้นพร้อมกัน
eng_definition: existing, happening, or done at the same time
example_sentence: The criminal was sentenced to serve three concurrent life terms in prison.

thai_meaning: ยอมรับหรือให้อภัยในสิ่งที่ผิด, เอาหูไปนาเอาตาไปไร่
eng_definition: accept and allow (behavior that is considered morally wrong or offensive) to continue
example_sentence: The university does not condone any form of academic dishonesty.

thai_meaning: มหาอัคคีภัย, ไฟไหม้ครั้งใหญ่
eng_definition: an extensive fire that destroys a great deal of land or property
example_sentence: The spark from the campfire quickly escalated into a devastating conflagration.

thai_meaning: จุดบรรจบกัน, การมารวมตัวกัน
eng_definition: the junction of two rivers, especially rivers of approximately equal width; an act or process of merging
example_sentence: The city is located at the confluence of two major trade routes.

thai_meaning: แข็งตัว, จับตัวเป็นก้อน
eng_definition: solidify or coagulate, especially by cooling
example_sentence: The blood had started to congeal on the victim's clothing.

thai_meaning: การคาดเดา, ข้อสันนิษฐานที่ยังไม่มีหลักฐานแน่ชัด
eng_definition: an opinion or conclusion formed on the basis of incomplete information
example_sentence: Without empirical data, his theory remains pure conjecture.

thai_meaning: มติเอกฉันท์, ความคิดเห็นพ้องต้องกันของคนส่วนใหญ่
eng_definition: a general agreement
example_sentence: The committee finally reached a consensus on the new budget allocation.

thai_meaning: บีบบังคับ, จำกัดให้อยู่ในกรอบ
eng_definition: severely restrict the scope, extent, or activity of
example_sentence: Economic factors currently constrain the government's ability to lower taxes.

thai_meaning: สมบูรณ์แบบ, เป็นเลิศในทักษะ
eng_definition: showing a high degree of skill and flair; complete or perfect
example_sentence: He played the piano with the elegance and grace of a consummate professional.

thai_meaning: ต่อสู้ดิ้นรน, ยืนยัน(ในการโต้เถียง)
eng_definition: struggle to surmount a difficulty or danger; assert something as a position in an argument
example_sentence: The lawyer will contend that his client was nowhere near the scene of the crime.

thai_meaning: ที่ขึ้นอยู่กับ (สิ่งอื่นที่อาจเกิดขึ้นหรือไม่ก็ได้), กลุ่มตัวแทน
eng_definition: subject to chance; occurring or existing only if certain other circumstances are the case
example_sentence: The merger is contingent upon approval by the regulatory authorities.

thai_meaning: มากมายก่ายกอง, อุดมสมบูรณ์
eng_definition: abundant in supply or quantity
example_sentence: She took copious notes during the extremely fast-paced lecture.

thai_meaning: ยืนยัน, สนับสนุนด้วยหลักฐาน
eng_definition: confirm or give support to (a statement, theory, or finding)
example_sentence: Recent scientific studies corroborate the theory that the earth's climate is rapidly changing.

thai_meaning: เป็นสากล, ประกอบด้วยผู้คนจากหลากหลายวัฒนธรรม
eng_definition: familiar with and at ease in many different countries and cultures
example_sentence: London is a highly cosmopolitan city with residents from all over the world.

thai_meaning: สีหน้า, ยอมรับหรือสนับสนุน
eng_definition: a person's face or facial expression; admit as acceptable or possible
example_sentence: The strict school principal would not countenance any form of bullying.

thai_meaning: แอบแฝง, ปิดบังซ่อนเร้น
eng_definition: not openly acknowledged or displayed
example_sentence: The intelligence agency conducted a covert operation to rescue the hostages.

thai_meaning: มีนัยซ่อนเร้น, ลึกลับตีความยาก
eng_definition: having a meaning that is mysterious or obscure
example_sentence: He left a cryptic message on my desk before suddenly quitting his job.

thai_meaning: บรรลุจุดสูงสุด, ลงเอยด้วย
eng_definition: reach a climax or point of highest development
example_sentence: The festival will culminate in a spectacular fireworks display over the bay.

thai_meaning: น่าตำหนิ, มีความผิด
eng_definition: deserving blame
example_sentence: The manager was held totally culpable for the devastating safety failure.

thai_meaning: อย่างลวกๆ, อย่างผิวเผิน (ไม่ได้ดูรายละเอียด)
eng_definition: hasty and therefore not thorough or detailed
example_sentence: He gave the report only a cursory glance before approving it.

thai_meaning: ตัดทอน, ทำให้สั้นลง, ลดลง
eng_definition: reduce in extent or quantity; impose a restriction on
example_sentence: The company had to sharply curtail its travel budget due to falling profits.

thai_meaning: มองโลกในแง่ร้าย (เชื่อว่าทุกคนทำเพื่อผลประโยชน์ของตนเอง)
eng_definition: believing that people are motivated by self-interest; distrustful of human sincerity or integrity
example_sentence: She has a cynical view of politics and believes all politicians are corrupt.

thai_meaning: ทำให้กลัว, ทำให้เสียขวัญหรือหมดกำลังใจ
eng_definition: make (someone) feel intimidated or apprehensive
example_sentence: The massive scale of the project did not daunt the ambitious young engineer.

thai_meaning: ทำให้อ่อนแอ, บั่นทอนกำลัง
eng_definition: make (someone) weak and infirm
example_sentence: The severe bout of flu completely debilitated him for over a week.

thai_meaning: เปิดโปงความเท็จ, หักล้างความเชื่อผิดๆ
eng_definition: expose the falseness or hollowness of (a myth, idea, or belief)
example_sentence: The documentary aims to debunk the popular myths surrounding the Bermuda Triangle.

thai_meaning: ความเคารพยำเกรง, การยอมโอนอ่อนผ่อนตาม
eng_definition: humble submission and respect
example_sentence: The young lawyer treated the veteran judge with the utmost deference.

thai_meaning: พรรณนา, อธิบายรายละเอียดอย่างชัดเจน, ตีกรอบ
eng_definition: describe or portray (something) precisely
example_sentence: The contract clearly delineates the rights and responsibilities of both parties.

thai_meaning: ทำให้หมดไป, ลดจำนวนลงอย่างมาก
eng_definition: use up the supply or resources of
example_sentence: Deforestation has severely depleted the country's natural timber reserves.

thai_meaning: ความเสื่อมทรามทางศีลธรรม, ความชั่วร้าย
eng_definition: moral corruption; wickedness
example_sentence: The horrific crimes revealed a level of depravity that shocked the entire nation.

thai_meaning: การไม่เห็นด้วย, การแสดงความไม่เห็นพ้อง
eng_definition: express disapproval of
example_sentence: He smiled in self-deprecation, downplaying his crucial role in the victory.

thai_meaning: หัวเราะเยาะ, ดูถูกเหยียดหยาม
eng_definition: express contempt for; ridicule
example_sentence: Critics derided his new movie as a complete waste of time and money.

thai_meaning: ที่ลอกเลียนแบบมา, ขาดความสร้างสรรค์ใหม่
eng_definition: imitative of the work of another person, and usually disapproved of for that reason
example_sentence: The artist's latest album felt highly derivative of 1980s pop music.

thai_meaning: สิ้นหวัง, หดหู่ใจอย่างหนัก
eng_definition: in low spirits from loss of hope or courage
example_sentence: She became deeply despondent after failing the university entrance exam.

thai_meaning: ทรราช, ผู้ปกครองที่ใช้อำนาจเผด็จการ
eng_definition: a ruler or other person who holds absolute power, typically one who exercises it in a cruel or oppressive way
example_sentence: The country was ruled by a brutal despot who crushed any sign of rebellion.

thai_meaning: แร้นแค้น, ไม่มีสิ่งของที่จำเป็นต่อการดำรงชีวิต
eng_definition: without the basic necessities of life
example_sentence: The war left thousands of families completely destitute and homeless.

thai_meaning: ไม่ปะติดปะต่อ, ทำแบบขอไปที, ไร้จุดหมาย
eng_definition: lacking a plan, purpose, or enthusiasm
example_sentence: He made a few desultory attempts to clean his room before giving up.

thai_meaning: ขัดขวาง, ยับยั้ง (ด้วยความกลัวหรือผลลัพธ์)
eng_definition: discourage (someone) from doing something by instilling doubt or fear of the consequences
example_sentence: The presence of security cameras is intended to deter shoplifters.

thai_meaning: ลดคุณค่า, ทำให้เสียอรรถรส
eng_definition: reduce or take away the worth or value of
example_sentence: The poor lighting in the museum detracted from the beauty of the paintings.

thai_meaning: เบี่ยงเบน, ออกนอกลู่นอกทาง
eng_definition: depart from an established course
example_sentence: We must not deviate from the original plan if we want to finish on time.

thai_meaning: มีเล่ห์เหลี่ยม, คดเคี้ยว, ไม่ซื่อตรง
eng_definition: showing a skillful use of underhanded tactics to achieve goals
example_sentence: He used devious methods to win the election, including bribing officials.

thai_meaning: คล่องแคล่ว, ชำนาญ (โดยเฉพาะการใช้มือ)
eng_definition: showing or having skill, especially with the hands
example_sentence: The dexterous surgeon completed the complicated operation flawlessly.

thai_meaning: การแบ่งแยกออกเป็นสองส่วนที่ขัดแย้งกันอย่างชัดเจน
eng_definition: a division or contrast between two things that are or are represented as being opposed or entirely different
example_sentence: There is a stark dichotomy between the wealth of the elites and the extreme poverty of the masses.

thai_meaning: เชิงสั่งสอน, มีจุดประสงค์เพื่อให้คติสอนใจ
eng_definition: intended to teach, particularly in having moral instruction as an ulterior motive
example_sentence: Children's books in the 19th century were highly didactic, focusing heavily on moral behavior.

thai_meaning: พูดนอกเรื่อง, ออกนอกประเด็นหลัก
eng_definition: leave the main subject temporarily in speech or writing
example_sentence: Please allow me to digress for a moment to explain the historical background of this issue.

thai_meaning: สังเกตเห็น, แยกแยะความแตกต่างได้
eng_definition: perceive or recognize (something)
example_sentence: It is difficult to discern any real difference between the two political candidates.

thai_meaning: เศร้าโศกอย่างไม่อาจปลอบโยนได้
eng_definition: without consolation or comfort; unhappy
example_sentence: The little boy was completely disconsolate after losing his favorite toy.

thai_meaning: ความไม่สอดคล้องกัน, ความคลาดเคลื่อน (ของข้อมูล)
eng_definition: a lack of compatibility or similarity between two or more facts
example_sentence: The auditors found a massive discrepancy between the company's reported profits and their actual bank balances.

thai_meaning: แยกจากกันโดยเด็ดขาด, ไม่ปะปนกัน
eng_definition: individually separate and distinct
example_sentence: The course is broken down into five discrete modules, each covering a different topic.

thai_meaning: แบ่งแยกปฏิบัติ, แยกแยะความแตกต่าง
eng_definition: make an unjust or prejudicial distinction in the treatment of different categories of people; recognize a distinction
example_sentence: It is illegal for employers to discriminate on the basis of race, religion, or gender.

thai_meaning: การดูถูกเหยียดหยาม, รังเกียจ
eng_definition: the feeling that someone or something is unworthy of one's consideration or respect
example_sentence: She glared at the rude customer with unconcealed disdain.

thai_meaning: เผยแพร่, กระจาย(ข้อมูลหรือความรู้)
eng_definition: spread (something, especially information) widely
example_sentence: The internet allows people to disseminate information across the globe in seconds.

thai_meaning: ความขัดแย้ง, การไม่เห็นด้วย (โดยเฉพาะกับความเห็นส่วนใหญ่)
eng_definition: the expression or holding of opinions at variance with those previously or commonly held
example_sentence: The authoritarian regime fiercely suppressed any political dissent.

thai_meaning: พองตัว, บวมเบ่ง, ขยายออก
eng_definition: swell or cause to swell by pressure from inside
example_sentence: Malnutrition can cause a child's stomach to severely distend.

thai_meaning: แยกออกไป, แตกต่างกันไป (ทางเดินหรือความคิด)
eng_definition: (of a road, route, or line) separate from another route and go in a different direction; (of opinions) differ
example_sentence: Although they started the business together, their views on its future eventually began to diverge.

thai_meaning: ที่ก่อให้เกิดความแตกแยก
eng_definition: tending to cause disagreement or hostility between people
example_sentence: Abortion remains a highly divisive issue in American politics.

thai_meaning: เปิดเผย(ความลับ)
eng_definition: make known (private or sensitive information)
example_sentence: The doctor refused to divulge any details about his patient's medical condition.

thai_meaning: อยู่ในช่วงพักตัว, ซบเซา, หลับใหล
eng_definition: (of an animal) having normal physical functions suspended or slowed down for a period of time; in or as if in a deep sleep
example_sentence: The seeds will remain dormant in the soil until the spring rains arrive.

thai_meaning: น่าสงสัย, เคลือบแคลง, ไม่น่าเชื่อถือ
eng_definition: hesitating or doubting; not to be relied upon; suspect
example_sentence: He made some highly dubious claims about his past work experience on his resume.

thai_meaning: แปลกประหลาด, ผิดปกติ, นอกคอก
eng_definition: unconventional and slightly strange
example_sentence: The eccentric billionaire built a mansion shaped like a giant spaceship.

thai_meaning: บดบังรัศมี, ทำให้ความสำคัญลดลง
eng_definition: to make something seem less important or successful; to obscure light
example_sentence: Her outstanding performance in the final match managed to eclipse her previous failures.

thai_meaning: อบรมสั่งสอน, พัฒนาจิตใจหรือศีลธรรม
eng_definition: to instruct or improve someone morally or intellectually
example_sentence: The pastor delivered a sermon intended to edify his congregation.

thai_meaning: ลบออก, ทำลายร่องรอย, ทำตัวไม่ให้เป็นที่สังเกต
eng_definition: to erase a mark from a surface; to make oneself appear insignificant
example_sentence: Time and weather had completely effaced the inscription on the ancient monument.

thai_meaning: แสดงความรู้สึกออกมามากเกินไป, พรั่งพรู
eng_definition: expressing feelings of gratitude, pleasure, or approval in an unrestrained or heartfelt manner
example_sentence: They gave the returning hero an effusive welcome at the airport.

thai_meaning: ผู้ที่เชื่อในความเสมอภาค, ความเท่าเทียมกัน
eng_definition: believing in the principle that all people are equal and deserve equal rights and opportunities
example_sentence: The new political party promotes an egalitarian society where wealth is distributed fairly.

thai_meaning: เลวร้ายอย่างเห็นได้ชัด, ร้ายแรงมาก
eng_definition: outstandingly bad; shocking
example_sentence: The auditor discovered an egregious error in the company's financial calculations.

thai_meaning: ดึงออกมา, ล้วง(ข้อมูลหรือความรู้สึก)
eng_definition: evoke or draw out a response, answer, or fact from someone in reaction to one's own actions
example_sentence: The detective used clever questioning to elicit a confession from the suspect.

thai_meaning: อธิบายให้กระจ่าง, ทำให้ชัดเจน
eng_definition: to make something clear; explain
example_sentence: Could you please elucidate the main points of your theoretical framework?

thai_meaning: หลบหลีกเก่ง, ยากที่จะหาหรือทำความเข้าใจ
eng_definition: difficult to find, catch, or achieve; difficult to remember or recall
example_sentence: The elusive snow leopard is rarely seen by humans in the wild.

thai_meaning: ตกแต่ง, ประดับประดา, แต่งเรื่องเติมสีสัน
eng_definition: make something more attractive by the addition of decorative details or features; add extra fictitious details to a story
example_sentence: He tends to embellish his travel stories to make them sound more exciting.

thai_meaning: ยักยอกเงิน, ฉ้อโกงเงินขององค์กร
eng_definition: steal or misappropriate money placed in one's trust or belonging to the organization for which one works
example_sentence: The accountant was arrested for attempting to embezzle millions of dollars from the charity.

thai_meaning: มีชื่อเสียง, โดดเด่น, เป็นที่เคารพยกย่อง (ในสายอาชีพ)
eng_definition: famous and respected within a particular sphere or profession
example_sentence: An eminent professor of physics was invited to give the keynote speech.

thai_meaning: ความเห็นอกเห็นใจ, การเอาใจเขามาใส่ใจเรา
eng_definition: the ability to understand and share the feelings of another
example_sentence: Nurses need to have a great deal of empathy when dealing with frightened patients.

thai_meaning: เลียนแบบ(เพื่อเอาอย่าง), พยายามทำให้เทียบเท่า
eng_definition: match or surpass a person or achievement, typically by imitation
example_sentence: Many young athletes try to emulate the success of their sporting idols.

thai_meaning: ครอบคลุม, รวมเข้าไว้ด้วยกัน
eng_definition: surround and have or hold within; include comprehensively
example_sentence: The new environmental curriculum will encompass both science and public policy.

thai_meaning: รุกล้ำ, ล่วงล้ำ(อาณาเขตหรือสิทธิ)
eng_definition: intrude on a person's territory, rights, personal life, etc.
example_sentence: Urban development continues to encroach upon the natural habitats of wildlife.

thai_meaning: เฉพาะถิ่น, มีอยู่ประจำในพื้นที่นั้นๆ
eng_definition: regularly found among particular people or in a certain area
example_sentence: Malaria is a disease that is endemic to many tropical regions.

thai_meaning: ปริศนา, สิ่งที่ลึกลับเข้าใจยาก
eng_definition: a person or thing that is mysterious, puzzling, or difficult to understand
example_sentence: How the ancient pyramids were built without modern machinery remains an enigma.

thai_meaning: ความเกลียดชัง, ความเป็นศัตรู
eng_definition: the state or feeling of being actively opposed or hostile to someone or something
example_sentence: Decades of political enmity existed between the two neighboring nations.

thai_meaning: ทำให้จำเป็นต้องมี, นำมาซึ่ง
eng_definition: involve something as a necessary or inevitable part or consequence
example_sentence: Running a successful startup will entail a lot of hard work and sleepless nights.

thai_meaning: สิ่งที่มีอยู่จริง, องค์กรหรือหน่วยงานที่เป็นเอกเทศ
eng_definition: a thing with distinct and independent existence
example_sentence: The newly acquired company will continue to operate as a separate entity.

thai_meaning: อ้อนวอน, ขอร้องอย่างจริงจัง
eng_definition: ask someone earnestly or anxiously to do something
example_sentence: I entreat you to reconsider your decision before it is too late.

thai_meaning: มีอายุสั้นมาก, ไม่จีรังยั่งยืน
eng_definition: lasting for a very short time
example_sentence: The beauty of a cherry blossom is ephemeral, lasting only a few days.

thai_meaning: ความสมดุล, ภาวะที่ลงตัว
eng_definition: a state in which opposing forces or influences are balanced
example_sentence: The sudden increase in demand upset the delicate economic equilibrium.

thai_meaning: ยุติธรรม, เสมอภาค
eng_definition: fair and impartial
example_sentence: The union demanded an equitable distribution of the company's profits among all workers.

thai_meaning: กำกวม, ตีความได้หลายแง่, ไม่แน่ชัด
eng_definition: open to more than one interpretation; ambiguous
example_sentence: The scientific results were equivocal, failing to prove or disprove the hypothesis.

thai_meaning: ถอนรากถอนโคน, กำจัดให้สิ้นซาก
eng_definition: destroy completely; put an end to
example_sentence: Global health organizations are working hard to completely eradicate polio.

thai_meaning: เอาแน่เอานอนไม่ได้, ผันผวน
eng_definition: not even or regular in pattern or movement; unpredictable
example_sentence: The stock market has shown highly erratic behavior over the past few weeks.

thai_meaning: ผิดพลาด, คลาดเคลื่อน
eng_definition: wrong; incorrect
example_sentence: The article was retracted because it was based on erroneous data.

thai_meaning: ทวีความรุนแรงขึ้น, ขยายตัวเพิ่มขึ้น
eng_definition: increase rapidly; become or cause to become more intense or serious
example_sentence: A minor border dispute can quickly escalate into a full-scale war.

thai_meaning: หลีกเลี่ยง, ละเว้น (ด้วยเหตุผลทางศีลธรรมหรือความเชื่อ)
eng_definition: deliberately avoid using; abstain from
example_sentence: Many monks choose to eschew modern conveniences to live a simple life.

thai_meaning: สนับสนุน, รับเอาแนวคิดหรืออุดมการณ์มาใช้
eng_definition: adopt or support a cause, belief, or way of life
example_sentence: The new politician was quick to espouse progressive social policies.

thai_meaning: ความเคารพยกย่อง, ประเมินค่า
eng_definition: respect and admiration, typically for a person
example_sentence: She is held in high esteem by her colleagues for her groundbreaking research.

thai_meaning: คำไว้อาลัย (มักกล่าวในงานศพ)
eng_definition: a speech or piece of writing that praises someone or something highly, typically someone who has just died
example_sentence: His best friend delivered a deeply moving eulogy at the funeral service.

thai_meaning: การใช้คำสละสลวยแทนคำที่ฟังดูหยาบหรือรุนแรง
eng_definition: a mild or indirect word or expression substituted for one considered to be too harsh or blunt
example_sentence: Using the phrase 'passed away' is a common euphemism for dying.

thai_meaning: ความรู้สึกปิติยินดีอย่างล้นหลาม
eng_definition: a feeling or state of intense excitement and happiness
example_sentence: Winning the championship brought a wave of euphoria over the entire city.

thai_meaning: มีลักษณะหลบเลี่ยง, บ่ายเบี่ยงไม่ตอบตรงๆ
eng_definition: tending to avoid commitment or self-revelation, especially by responding only indirectly
example_sentence: The CEO was heavily criticized for giving evasive answers during the press conference.

thai_meaning: ยกย่องสรรเสริญ, เลื่อนตำแหน่งให้สูงขึ้น
eng_definition: hold someone or something in very high regard; think or speak very highly of
example_sentence: The ancient poems exist mainly to exalt the bravery of legendary warriors.

thai_meaning: ทำให้โกรธหรือหงุดหงิดอย่างมาก
eng_definition: irritate intensely; infuriate
example_sentence: His constant complaining and negative attitude exasperate everyone in the office.

thai_meaning: ข้อความที่ตัดตอนมา, บางส่วนของเนื้อหา
eng_definition: a short extract from a film, broadcast, or piece of music or writing
example_sentence: The professor read an excerpt from Shakespeare's play to illustrate his point.

thai_meaning: ตัดออก(ด้วยการผ่าตัด หรือข้อความ), ภาษีสรรพสามิต
eng_definition: charge excise on goods; cut out surgically
example_sentence: The editor decided to excise the controversial paragraph from the final publication.

thai_meaning: เป็นแบบอย่างที่ดีเยี่ยม, น่ายกย่อง
eng_definition: representing the best of its kind; serving as a desirable model
example_sentence: She received a prestigious award for her exemplary service to the community.

thai_meaning: เป็นตัวอย่างที่ดีของ..., แสดงให้เห็นภาพชัดเจน
eng_definition: be a typical example of
example_sentence: The Renaissance period is widely known to exemplify a profound revival of art and literature.

thai_meaning: ละเอียดถี่ถ้วน, ครอบคลุมทุกแง่มุม
eng_definition: examining, including, or considering all elements or aspects; fully comprehensive
example_sentence: The police conducted an exhaustive search of the area but found nothing.

thai_meaning: ทำให้ร่าเริงเบิกบานใจ, ทำให้ตื่นเต้นดีใจสุดๆ
eng_definition: make someone feel very happy, animated, or elated
example_sentence: Riding the massive roller coaster was designed to exhilarate the amusement park guests.

thai_meaning: คะยั้นคะยอ, กระตุ้นเตือนอย่างแรงกล้า
eng_definition: strongly encourage or urge someone to do something
example_sentence: The governor took to the television to exhort citizens to evacuate immediately.

thai_meaning: แพงหูฉี่, (ราคา) สูงเกินกว่าเหตุมาก
eng_definition: (of a price or amount charged) unreasonably high
example_sentence: The luxury hotel charged an exorbitant rate for a simple bottle of water.

thai_meaning: กว้างขวาง, ครอบคลุมพื้นที่มาก, เปิดเผยเข้ากับคนง่าย
eng_definition: covering a wide area in terms of space or scope; open, demonstrative, and communicative
example_sentence: The estate featured expansive lawns that stretched all the way to the lake.

thai_meaning: ผู้ที่อาศัยอยู่ในต่างประเทศ, เนรเทศ
eng_definition: a person who lives outside their native country
example_sentence: Bangkok has a large community of expatriate teachers from around the world.

thai_meaning: ที่ได้ประโยชน์ชั่วคราว, เหมาะเจาะในสถานการณ์นั้น (แม้อาจไม่ถูกต้องตามหลักการ)
eng_definition: convenient and practical although possibly improper or immoral
example_sentence: The government found it expedient to ignore the human rights violations for the sake of trade.

thai_meaning: เร่งความเร็ว, ทำให้เสร็จเร็วขึ้น
eng_definition: make an action or process happen sooner or be accomplished more quickly
example_sentence: You can pay an extra fee to expedite the shipping of your online order.

thai_meaning: ความเชี่ยวชาญ, ทักษะเฉพาะทาง
eng_definition: expert skill or knowledge in a particular field
example_sentence: The project requires technical expertise that our current team simply does not possess.

thai_meaning: ชัดเจน, แจ่มแจ้ง, ไม่ปิดบัง
eng_definition: stated clearly and in detail, leaving no room for confusion or doubt
example_sentence: The teacher gave explicit instructions on how to format the final essay.

thai_meaning: เอาเปรียบ, ใช้ประโยชน์ (ในทางที่ไม่ดีหรือทางบวก)
eng_definition: make full use of and derive benefit from a resource; use a situation or person in an unfair or selfish way
example_sentence: Unscrupulous employers often exploit immigrant workers by paying them below minimum wage.

thai_meaning: ผู้สนับสนุนตัวยง, เลขชี้กำลัง
eng_definition: a person who believes in and promotes the truth or benefits of an idea or theory
example_sentence: She was a leading exponent of the new educational reform movement.

thai_meaning: ลบออกให้หมด, ขีดฆ่าทิ้ง
eng_definition: erase or remove completely something unwanted or unpleasant
example_sentence: The judge ordered that the juvenile offense be expunged from his permanent record.

thai_meaning: ชำระ(หนังสือหรือบทละคร)โดยการตัดเนื้อหาที่ไม่เหมาะสมออก
eng_definition: remove matter thought to be objectionable or unsuitable from a book or account
example_sentence: The publisher decided to expurgate the novel's violent scenes before releasing it to schools.

thai_meaning: ยังคงมีอยู่, ยังหลงเหลืออยู่ (มักใช้กับเอกสารโบราณ)
eng_definition: still in existence; surviving
example_sentence: This is the oldest extant manuscript of the philosopher's original work.

thai_meaning: ที่พูดหรือทำโดยไม่ได้เตรียมตัวมาก่อน
eng_definition: spoken or done without preparation
example_sentence: When the speaker fell ill, the principal gave an extemporaneous speech to fill the time.

thai_meaning: บรรเทาโทษ, ทำให้ความผิดดูเบาลง
eng_definition: make guilt or an offense seem less serious or more forgivable
example_sentence: His youth and inexperience may extenuate his foolish actions in the eyes of the court.

thai_meaning: สรรเสริญเยินยอ, ยกย่องอย่างมาก
eng_definition: praise enthusiastically
example_sentence: Health experts continuously extol the virtues of eating fresh vegetables daily.

thai_meaning: กรรโชกทรัพย์, รีดไถ
eng_definition: obtain something by force, threats, or other unfair means
example_sentence: The gang attempted to extort protection money from local business owners.

thai_meaning: ไม่เกี่ยวข้อง, เป็นส่วนเกิน
eng_definition: irrelevant or unrelated to the subject being dealt with
example_sentence: Please stick to the main topic and leave out any extraneous details.

thai_meaning: คาดการณ์(จากข้อมูลที่มีอยู่)
eng_definition: extend the application of a method or conclusion to an unknown situation by assuming that existing trends will continue
example_sentence: It is difficult to accurately extrapolate long-term trends from such a short period of data.

thai_meaning: ฟุ่มเฟือย, สุรุ่ยสุร่าย, เวอร์เกินจริง
eng_definition: lacking restraint in spending money or using resources; exceeding what is reasonable or appropriate
example_sentence: She threw an extravagant party that must have cost thousands of dollars.

thai_meaning: ช่วยให้รอดพ้น, แกะออก(จากสถานการณ์ยุ่งยาก)
eng_definition: free someone or something from a constraint or difficulty
example_sentence: The rescue team worked for hours to extricate the driver from the wrecked car.

thai_meaning: ที่มาจากภายนอก, ไม่ใช่เนื้อแท้
eng_definition: not part of the essential nature of someone or something; coming or operating from outside
example_sentence: Money is an extrinsic motivator that can sometimes undermine genuine passion for a job.

thai_meaning: ความร่าเริงมีชีวิตชีวา, ความอุดมสมบูรณ์
eng_definition: the quality of being full of energy, excitement, and cheerfulness
example_sentence: The children's sheer exuberance at the playground was contagious.

thai_meaning: ปิติยินดี, ลิงโลดใจ (โดยเฉพาะจากชัยชนะ)
eng_definition: show or feel elation or jubilation, especially as the result of a success
example_sentence: The fans began to exult loudly as their team scored the final winning goal.

thai_meaning: ปลอมแปลง, กุเรื่อง, ประดิษฐ์ขึ้น
eng_definition: invent or concoct something, typically with deceitful intent; construct or manufacture
example_sentence: He was fired after the company discovered he had fabricated the research data.

thai_meaning: หน้าฉากที่หลอกตา, ด้านหน้าของตึก
eng_definition: the face of a building; an outward appearance that is maintained to conceal a less pleasant reality
example_sentence: Despite his calm facade, he was actually panicking inside.

thai_meaning: ทำให้ง่ายขึ้น, อำนวยความสะดวก
eng_definition: make an action or process easy or easier
example_sentence: The new airport expansion will facilitate international trade and tourism.

thai_meaning: ฝักฝ่าย, กลุ่มย่อย(ที่มีความขัดแย้งในกลุ่มใหญ่)
eng_definition: a small organized dissenting group within a larger one, especially in politics
example_sentence: A rebel faction within the political party threatened to vote against the leadership.

thai_meaning: ความเชื่อที่ผิด, ตรรกะวิบัติ
eng_definition: a mistaken belief, especially one based on unsound argument
example_sentence: It is a common fallacy that lightning never strikes the same place twice.

thai_meaning: สะดุด, ลังเล, เริ่มสูญเสียความมั่นใจหรือความแข็งแรง
eng_definition: start to lose strength or momentum; speak or move hesitantly
example_sentence: The economic recovery began to falter as inflation rose sharply.

thai_meaning: ผู้คลั่งไคล้รุนแรง, ผู้มีอุดมการณ์สุดโต่ง
eng_definition: a person filled with excessive and single-minded zeal, especially for an extreme religious or political cause
example_sentence: The security team stopped a religious fanatic from disrupting the peaceful ceremony.

thai_meaning: เรื่องตลกขบขันไร้สาระ, การกระทำที่ล้มเหลวแบบน่าสมเพช
eng_definition: a comic dramatic work using buffoonery and horseplay; an absurd event
example_sentence: The debate turned into a complete farce when the candidates started throwing papers at each other.

thai_meaning: หยั่งรู้, ทำความเข้าใจอย่างถ่องแท้
eng_definition: understand a difficult problem or an enigmatic person after much thought
example_sentence: I simply cannot fathom why she would quit such a high-paying job.

thai_meaning: ที่เป็นไปได้, สามารถทำได้จริง
eng_definition: possible to do easily or conveniently
example_sentence: The committee concluded that building a new bridge over the river was not financially feasible.

thai_meaning: เสแสร้ง, แกล้งทำ
eng_definition: pretend to be affected by a feeling, state, or injury
example_sentence: He tried to feign sickness to avoid taking his final exams.

thai_meaning: ความสุขุมลุ่มลึก, ความสุขอย่างยิ่ง, ความเหมาะสมเจาะจง
eng_definition: intense happiness; the ability to find appropriate expression for one's thoughts
example_sentence: She expressed her gratitude with great felicity and charm.

thai_meaning: เร่าร้อน, กระตือรือร้นอย่างแรงกล้า
eng_definition: having or displaying a passionate intensity
example_sentence: The activist is a fervent supporter of animal rights and environmental protection.

thai_meaning: กลัดหนอง, (ปัญหา) รุนแรงขึ้นเพราะถูกปล่อยทิ้งไว้
eng_definition: become infected; (of a negative feeling or problem) become worse or more intense, especially through long-term neglect
example_sentence: If you don't communicate your frustrations, your anger will only fester over time.

thai_meaning: น่าเกรงขาม, รับมือยาก
eng_definition: inspiring fear or respect through being impressively large, powerful, intense, or capable
example_sentence: The defending champion is a formidable opponent who has never lost a match.

thai_meaning: ละทิ้ง, ทอดทิ้ง
eng_definition: abandon someone or something; renounce or give up something valued or pleasant
example_sentence: He promised his wife that he would never forsake her, no matter how hard life got.

thai_meaning: บังเอิญโชคดี
eng_definition: happening by a lucky chance; fortunate
example_sentence: The meeting with the wealthy investor was a fortuitous coincidence for the struggling startup.

thai_meaning: อุปถัมภ์, สนับสนุนให้เกิดการพัฒนา
eng_definition: encourage or promote the development of something, typically something regarded as good
