example_sentence: The teacher strives to foster a creative and open-minded environment in her classroom.

thai_meaning: ที่หลอกลวงต้มตุ๋น, ทุจริต
eng_definition: obtained, done by, or involving deception, especially criminal deception
example_sentence: The bank blocked the transaction due to suspected fraudulent activity on the account.

thai_meaning: เต็มไปด้วย (ปัญหามักใช้ในแง่ลบ), ตึงเครียด
eng_definition: filled with or destined to result in something undesirable; causing or affected by great anxiety or stress
example_sentence: The negotiation process was fraught with difficulties from the very beginning.

thai_meaning: บ้าคลั่ง, วุ่นวายอย่างรวดเร็ว
eng_definition: fast and energetic in a rather wild and uncontrolled way
example_sentence: The kitchen was in a frenetic state as the chefs prepared for the grand opening.

thai_meaning: ไร้สาระ, ไม่เอาจริงเอาจัง
eng_definition: not having any serious purpose or value; carefree and superficial
example_sentence: The judge dismissed the lawsuit, calling it a completely frivolous waste of the court's time.

thai_meaning: ประหยัดมัธยัสถ์
eng_definition: sparing or economical with regard to money or food
example_sentence: Growing up during the depression taught him to be extremely frugal with his money.

thai_meaning: ลับๆ ล่อๆ, มีพิรุธ
eng_definition: attempting to avoid notice or attention, typically because of guilt or a belief that discovery would lead to trouble
example_sentence: The student cast a furtive glance at his neighbor's exam paper.

thai_meaning: ไร้ประโยชน์, ป่วยการ
eng_definition: incapable of producing any useful result; pointless
example_sentence: All efforts to revive the dying plant proved futile.

thai_meaning: กระตุ้นให้เกิดการกระทำ, ปลุกระดม
eng_definition: shock or excite someone into taking action
example_sentence: The urgent climate report helped to galvanize the public into demanding immediate political reform.

thai_meaning: บิดเบือนเนื้อหา(จนฟังไม่รู้เรื่อง), ทำให้สับสน
eng_definition: reproduce a message, sound, or transmission in a confused and distorted way
example_sentence: The bad phone connection caused his important message to become completely garbled.

thai_meaning: รวบรวม, ได้รับ (ข้อมูลหรือเสียงสนับสนุน)
eng_definition: gather or collect something, especially information or approval
example_sentence: The documentary managed to garner widespread critical acclaim and several prestigious awards.

thai_meaning: ผอมโซ, ซูบซีด (เพราะป่วยหรืออดอยาก)
eng_definition: lean and haggard, especially because of suffering, hunger, or age
example_sentence: After weeks lost in the wilderness, the hiker returned looking pale and gaunt.

thai_meaning: โดยทั่วไป, ไม่มียี่ห้อ, ธรรมดาสามัญ
eng_definition: characteristic of or relating to a class or group of things; not specific; lacking imagination or individuality
example_sentence: The doctor prescribed a generic equivalent of the medication to save the patient money.

thai_meaning: ตรงประเด็น, เกี่ยวเนื่องกัน
eng_definition: relevant to a subject under consideration
example_sentence: The lawyer asked the witness to keep her testimony germane to the case.

thai_meaning: องค์รวม (ที่ยิ่งใหญ่กว่าผลรวมของส่วนย่อย)
eng_definition: an organized whole that is perceived as more than the sum of its parts
example_sentence: We need to look at the gestalt of the problem rather than just isolated incidents.

thai_meaning: พูดคล่องแต่ไม่จริงใจ, กะล่อน
eng_definition: (of words or the person speaking them) fluent and voluble but insincere and shallow
example_sentence: The politician's glib answers failed to satisfy the concerned journalists.

thai_meaning: ยั่วยุ, กระตุ้นให้ทำ (มักด้วยความรำคาญ)
eng_definition: provoke or annoy (someone) so as to stimulate some action or reaction
example_sentence: The older brother tried to goad him into a fight, but he refused to react.

thai_meaning: บางเบา, อ่อนช้อยราวกับใยแมงมุม
eng_definition: used to refer to something very light, thin, and insubstantial or delicate
example_sentence: She wore a dress made of gossamer silk that floated beautifully in the wind.

thai_meaning: ขูดรีด, โก่งราคาอย่างไม่เป็นธรรม
eng_definition: overcharge; swindle
example_sentence: Many stores were accused of trying to gouge customers during the natural disaster.

thai_meaning: การใช้ถ้อยคำหรูหราโอ้อวด (เพื่อโชว์ภูมิ)
eng_definition: a lofty, extravagantly colorful, pompous, or bombastic style, manner, or quality especially in language
example_sentence: The professor's grandiloquence made his lectures very difficult for freshmen to understand.

thai_meaning: ยิ่งใหญ่ตระการตา, โอ้อวดเกินจริง
eng_definition: impressive or magnificent in appearance or style, especially pretentiously so
example_sentence: He presented a grandiose plan to rebuild the entire city center in just one year.

thai_meaning: ทำไปโดยไม่มีเหตุผลอันควร, ให้เปล่า (มักใช้ในแง่ลบ เช่น ความรุนแรงเกินจำเป็น)
eng_definition: uncalled for; lacking good reason; unwarranted
example_sentence: The movie was criticized for its gratuitous violence that added nothing to the plot.

thai_meaning: รุนแรงมาก, สาหัส, ทำให้เศร้าโศก
eng_definition: (of something bad) very severe or serious
example_sentence: The general's tactical error was a grievous mistake that cost many lives.

thai_meaning: หมอบกราบ, ประจบสอพลออย่างไร้ศักดิ์ศรี
eng_definition: act in an obsequious manner in order to obtain someone's forgiveness or favor
example_sentence: He had to grovel to his boss to get his job back after the massive failure.

thai_meaning: มารยา, เล่ห์เหลี่ยม
eng_definition: sly or cunning intelligence
example_sentence: The seasoned politician used his guile to outmaneuver his younger opponents.

thai_meaning: หูเบา, ถูกหลอกง่าย
eng_definition: easily persuaded to believe something; credulous
example_sentence: Scammers often target gullible people with fake investment schemes.

thai_meaning: โชคร้าย, เคราะห์ร้าย
eng_definition: (especially of a person) unfortunate
example_sentence: The hapless passengers were stranded at the airport for three days.

thai_meaning: ลางบอกเหตุ, ผู้แจ้งข่าวล่วงหน้า
eng_definition: a person or thing that announces or signals the approach of another
example_sentence: The sudden drop in temperature was a harbinger of the approaching blizzard.

thai_meaning: หยิ่งยโส, จองหอง
eng_definition: arrogantly superior and disdainful
example_sentence: The aristocrat cast a haughty look at the servants cleaning the hall.

thai_meaning: ผู้ที่ยึดถือความสุขสำราญเป็นเป้าหมายสูงสุดในชีวิต
eng_definition: a person who believes that the pursuit of pleasure is the most important thing in life
example_sentence: Living as a wealthy hedonist, he spent all his time partying and traveling.

thai_meaning: ชั่วร้ายมาก, เลวทราม (มักใช้กับอาชญากรรม)
eng_definition: (of a person or wrongful act, especially a crime) utterly odious or wicked
example_sentence: The criminal was sentenced to life in prison for his heinous acts.

thai_meaning: ความเชื่อนอกรีต, การขัดแย้งกับความเชื่อหลัก
eng_definition: belief or opinion contrary to orthodox religious (especially Christian) doctrine
example_sentence: In the Middle Ages, suggesting that the Earth revolved around the Sun was considered heresy.

thai_meaning: การหยุดชะงักชั่วคราว, ช่องว่าง
eng_definition: a pause or gap in a sequence, series, or process
example_sentence: After a three-year hiatus, the band finally released a new album.

thai_meaning: ระบบลำดับชั้น (ทางอำนาจหรือฐานะ)
eng_definition: a system or organization in which people or groups are ranked one above the other according to status or authority
example_sentence: The military operates strictly on a rigid hierarchy of command.

thai_meaning: บทเทศน์, การอบรมสั่งสอนศีลธรรม
eng_definition: a religious discourse that is intended primarily for spiritual edification rather than doctrinal instruction; a sermon
example_sentence: The priest delivered a brief homily on the importance of forgiveness.

thai_meaning: ที่เป็นเนื้อเดียวกัน, ที่มีลักษณะเหมือนกันหมด
eng_definition: of the same kind; alike
example_sentence: The neighborhood has a very homogeneous population, mostly consisting of young professionals.

thai_meaning: การพูดเกินจริง (เพื่อเน้นความรู้สึก)
eng_definition: exaggerated statements or claims not meant to be taken literally
example_sentence: Saying you're so hungry you could eat a horse is a classic example of hyperbole.

thai_meaning: หน้าไหว้หลังหลอก, มือถือสากปากถือศีล
eng_definition: behaving in a way that suggests one has higher standards or more noble beliefs than is the case
example_sentence: It is hypocritical of him to criticize her for being late when he is never on time himself.

thai_meaning: ผิดกฎหมาย, ผิดศีลธรรม
eng_definition: forbidden by law, rules, or custom
example_sentence: The police cracked down on the illicit trade of endangered animal species.

thai_meaning: ซึมซาบ, ปลูกฝัง (ความรู้สึกหรือคุณสมบัติ)
eng_definition: inspire or permeate with a feeling or quality
example_sentence: A good teacher should aim to imbue students with a lifelong love of learning.

thai_meaning: ไร้ที่ติ, สะอาดบริสุทธิ์อย่างยิ่ง
eng_definition: (especially of a person or their clothes) perfectly clean, neat, or tidy; free from flaws
example_sentence: Despite the long flight, she arrived wearing an immaculate white suit.

thai_meaning: ที่กำลังจะเกิดขึ้นเร็วๆ นี้, จวนตัว
eng_definition: about to happen
example_sentence: The dark clouds and high winds indicated that a storm was imminent.

thai_meaning: ทำให้เสื่อมเสีย, ทำให้แย่ลง
eng_definition: weaken or damage something (especially a human faculty or function)
example_sentence: Drinking alcohol will seriously impair your ability to drive safely.

thai_meaning: ทางตัน, สถานการณ์ที่หาทางออกไม่ได้
eng_definition: a situation in which no progress is possible, especially because of disagreement; a deadlock
example_sentence: The negotiations reached an impasse when neither side was willing to compromise.

thai_meaning: ขัดขวาง, ถ่วงความเจริญ
eng_definition: delay or prevent (someone or something) by obstructing them; hinder
example_sentence: The dense fog severely impeded the rescue team's progress up the mountain.

thai_meaning: เป็นสิ่งจำเป็นยิ่ง, ที่หลีกเลี่ยงไม่ได้
eng_definition: of vital importance; crucial
example_sentence: It is absolutely imperative that we finish this project before the deadline.

thai_meaning: ที่สังเกตเห็นได้ยากมาก, เล็กน้อยมาก
eng_definition: impossible to perceive
example_sentence: The change in the patient's heart rate was almost imperceptible to the naked eye.

thai_meaning: ที่บอกเป็นนัย, ที่เป็นที่เข้าใจกันโดยไม่ต้องพูด
eng_definition: implied though not plainly expressed
example_sentence: There was an implicit agreement that neither of them would mention the incident again.

thai_meaning: พังทลายลงจากภายใน, ยุบตัวลง
eng_definition: collapse or cause to collapse violently inward
example_sentence: The company's stock value began to implode after the massive fraud was exposed.

thai_meaning: ไม่ได้เตรียมตัวมาก่อน, กะทันหัน
eng_definition: done without being planned, organized, or rehearsed
example_sentence: He gave an excellent impromptu speech at the wedding reception.

thai_meaning: ทะลึ่ง, ไร้ยางอาย, ไม่ให้ความเคารพ
eng_definition: not showing due respect for another person; impertinent
example_sentence: The impudent child stuck his tongue out at the teacher.

thai_meaning: กล่าวหา, โยนความผิดให้
eng_definition: represent (something, especially something undesirable) as being done, caused, or possessed by someone; attribute
example_sentence: It is unfair to impute malicious motives to her when she simply made a mistake.

thai_meaning: ไม่ได้ตั้งใจ, เผลอ
eng_definition: not resulting from or achieved through deliberate planning
example_sentence: An inadvertent error in the code caused the entire website to crash.

thai_meaning: โง่เขลา, ไร้สาระ
eng_definition: silly; stupid
example_sentence: I cannot stand listening to their inane chatter about reality TV shows.

thai_meaning: ทำพิธีเปิด, เข้ารับตำแหน่งอย่างเป็นทางการ
eng_definition: begin or introduce (a system, policy, or period); admit (someone) formally to public office
example_sentence: The new president will be inaugurated in a grand ceremony next week.

thai_meaning: สิ่งจูงใจ, รางวัลกระตุ้น
eng_definition: a thing that motivates or encourages one to do something
example_sentence: The company offered a cash incentive to employees who exceeded their sales targets.

thai_meaning: ไม่หยุดหย่อน, ต่อเนื่องเรื่อยไป (มักน่ารำคาญ)
eng_definition: (of something regarded as unpleasant) continuing without pause or interruption
example_sentence: The incessant noise from the construction site gave me a terrible headache.

thai_meaning: ปลุกปั่น, ยั่วยุให้เกิดเรื่อง
eng_definition: encourage or stir up (violent or unlawful behavior)
example_sentence: He was arrested for attempting to incite a riot among the protesters.

thai_meaning: โดยปกปิดฐานะ, อำพรางตัว
eng_definition: (of a person) having one's true identity concealed
example_sentence: The famous actor traveled incognito to avoid being mobbed by fans.

thai_meaning: ไม่เข้ากัน, ดูขัดแย้งกับสิ่งรอบข้าง
eng_definition: not in harmony or keeping with the surroundings or other aspects of something
example_sentence: The modern glass building looked completely incongruous next to the medieval cathedral.

thai_meaning: ไม่สำคัญ, ขี้ปะติ๋ว
eng_definition: not important or significant
example_sentence: His personal opinions are absolutely inconsequential to the final decision.

thai_meaning: รวมเข้าด้วยกัน, ผสมผสาน
eng_definition: take in or contain (something) as part of a whole; include
example_sentence: We will try to incorporate your suggestions into the new software update.

thai_meaning: ที่ดัดนิสัยไม่ได้, เกินเยียวยา
eng_definition: (of a person or their tendencies) not able to be corrected, improved, or reformed
example_sentence: He is an incorrigible liar who simply cannot tell the truth.

thai_meaning: ไม่เชื่อ, กังขา
eng_definition: (of a person or their manner) unwilling or unable to believe something
example_sentence: She gave me an incredulous look when I told her I had won the lottery.

thai_meaning: การเพิ่มขึ้น (ทีละนิด), ส่วนเพิ่ม
eng_definition: an increase or addition, especially one of a series on a fixed scale
example_sentence: Employees receive a salary increment every year based on their performance.

thai_meaning: ทำให้ดูเหมือนมีความผิด, ซัดทอด
eng_definition: make (someone) appear guilty of a crime or wrongdoing; strongly imply the guilt of (someone)
example_sentence: He refused to answer the detective's questions so that he wouldn't incriminate himself.

thai_meaning: ฟัก(ไข่), เพาะเชื้อ, บ่มเพาะ(ความคิดหรือโครงการ)
eng_definition: keep (eggs, cells, bacteria, embryos, etc.) at a suitable temperature so that they develop; develop slowly
example_sentence: The virus can incubate in the body for up to two weeks before symptoms appear.

thai_meaning: ปลูกฝัง(ความคิดหรือนิสัย)โดยการย้ำเตือนบ่อยๆ
eng_definition: instill (an attitude, idea, or habit) by persistent instruction
example_sentence: The coach worked hard to inculcate a sense of teamwork and discipline in the players.

thai_meaning: ผู้ดำรงตำแหน่งปัจจุบัน, เป็นหน้าที่รับผิดชอบ
eng_definition: the holder of an office or post; necessary for (someone) as a duty or responsibility
example_sentence: It is incumbent upon the government to protect the basic rights of its citizens.

thai_meaning: ลบไม่ออก, ลืมไม่ลง
eng_definition: (of ink or a pen) making marks that cannot be removed; not able to be forgotten or removed
example_sentence: The tragic accident left an indelible mark on his memory forever.

thai_meaning: ฟ้องร้องคดีอาญา, กล่าวหาเป็นทางการ
eng_definition: formally accuse of or charge with a serious crime
example_sentence: The grand jury decided to indict the mayor on charges of corruption.

thai_meaning: พื้นเมือง, ดั้งเดิมในท้องถิ่น
eng_definition: originating or occurring naturally in a particular place; native
example_sentence: The kangaroo is indigenous only to the continent of Australia.

thai_meaning: ขุ่นเคือง (เพราะรู้สึกว่าไม่ยุติธรรม)
eng_definition: feeling or showing anger or annoyance at what is perceived as unfair treatment
example_sentence: She wrote an indignant letter to the company after receiving incredibly poor service.

thai_meaning: ไม่ยอมพ่ายแพ้, ทรหดอดทนอย่างยิ่ง
eng_definition: impossible to subdue or defeat
example_sentence: Despite losing his eyesight, his indomitable spirit allowed him to become a successful author.

thai_meaning: แน่ชัด, ไม่มีข้อกังขา
eng_definition: impossible to doubt; unquestionable
example_sentence: The DNA evidence provided indubitable proof of the suspect's guilt.

thai_meaning: ชักนำ, ก่อให้เกิด
eng_definition: succeed in persuading or influencing (someone) to do something; bring about or give rise to
example_sentence: The doctor had to induce labor because the baby was two weeks overdue.

thai_meaning: ลึกซึ้งจนเกินกว่าจะบรรยายได้, ไม่สามารถอธิบายเป็นคำพูดได้
eng_definition: too great or extreme to be expressed or described in words
example_sentence: Looking at the vast, starry sky gave her an ineffable sense of wonder.

thai_meaning: ไม่ชำนาญ, งุ่มง่าม, ไร้ความสามารถ
eng_definition: having or showing no skill; clumsy
example_sentence: His inept handling of the PR crisis only made the public angrier.

thai_meaning: อยู่นิ่งเฉย, ไร้ปฏิกิริยา(ทางเคมี), ไม่มีชีวิตชีวา
eng_definition: lacking the ability or strength to move; chemically inactive
example_sentence: Helium is an inert gas, meaning it does not readily react with other elements.

thai_meaning: ไม่มีวันผิดพลาด, ถูกต้องเสมอ
eng_definition: incapable of making mistakes or being wrong
example_sentence: No diagnostic test is absolutely infallible; false positives do occur.

thai_meaning: ฉาวโฉ่, มีชื่อเสียงในทางเลวร้าย
eng_definition: well known for some bad quality or deed
example_sentence: He is infamous for being one of the most ruthless dictators in modern history.

thai_meaning: การอนุมาน, ข้อสรุปที่ได้จากการคิดตรรกะ
eng_definition: a conclusion reached on the basis of evidence and reasoning
example_sentence: From his gloomy expression, the obvious inference was that he failed the exam.

thai_meaning: นรก, กองเพลิงขนาดใหญ่ที่ควบคุมไม่ได้
eng_definition: a large fire that is dangerously out of control
example_sentence: By the time the firefighters arrived, the warehouse was a raging inferno.

thai_meaning: เล็กน้อยมากๆ, จิ๋วหลิว
eng_definition: extremely small
example_sentence: The chances of winning the lottery are purely infinitesimal.

thai_meaning: ความอ่อนแอ(ทางร่างกายหรือจิตใจ), อาการเจ็บป่วย
eng_definition: physical or mental weakness
example_sentence: Due to his advanced age and infirmity, he could no longer live alone.

thai_meaning: การไหลทะลักเข้ามา(ของคนหรือสิ่งของ)
eng_definition: an arrival or entry of large numbers of people or things
example_sentence: The discovery of gold led to a massive influx of prospectors to the region.

thai_meaning: การละเมิดกฎ, การทำผิดกฎหมาย(ระดับเบา)
eng_definition: a violation or infringement of a law, agreement, or set of rules
example_sentence: Speeding is considered a minor traffic infraction, but it still incurs a fine.

thai_meaning: ละเมิด(สิทธิหรือกฎ), รุกล้ำ
eng_definition: actively break the terms of a law, agreement, etc.; act so as to limit or undermine something
example_sentence: Making illegal copies of the software is an attempt to infringe on copyright laws.

thai_meaning: ทำให้โกรธจัด
eng_definition: make (someone) extremely angry and impatient
example_sentence: It infuriates me when people text on their phones while driving.

thai_meaning: เทใส่, แช่ให้ซึมซาบ, เติมเต็ม(ความรู้สึกหรือคุณสมบัติ)
eng_definition: fill; pervade; soak (tea, herbs, etc.) in liquid to extract the flavor or healing properties
example_sentence: The new manager sought to infuse the team with a renewed sense of purpose.

thai_meaning: ฉลาดหลักแหลม, ช่างคิดช่างประดิษฐ์
eng_definition: (of a person) clever, original, and inventive
example_sentence: She came up with an ingenious solution to fix the complicated machinery.

thai_meaning: ฝังรากลึก(นิสัยหรือความเชื่อ), ฝังแน่น
eng_definition: (of a habit, belief, or attitude) firmly fixed or established; difficult to change
example_sentence: The idea of working hard was deeply ingrained in him from a young age.

thai_meaning: คนอกตัญญู
eng_definition: an ungrateful person
example_sentence: After all the help I gave him, he acted like an ingrate and never even said thank you.

thai_meaning: ประจบประแจง, ทำให้ผู้อื่นพอใจเพื่อผลประโยชน์
eng_definition: bring oneself into favor with someone by flattering or trying to please them
example_sentence: He tried to ingratiate himself with the boss by constantly bringing her coffee.

thai_meaning: ที่เป็นเนื้อแท้, ที่มีอยู่เป็นปกติวิสัย
eng_definition: existing in something as a permanent, essential, or characteristic attribute
example_sentence: There are inherent risks in starting any new business venture.

thai_meaning: ริเริ่ม, เริ่มต้น
eng_definition: cause (a process or action) to begin
example_sentence: The government plans to initiate a new healthcare program next year.

thai_meaning: คำสั่งศาล (ให้กระทำหรืองดเว้นการกระทำ)
eng_definition: an authoritative warning or order, especially a judicial order
example_sentence: The company sought a court injunction to prevent the workers from going on strike.

thai_meaning: ที่มีติดตัวมาแต่กำเนิด
eng_definition: inborn; natural
example_sentence: Mozart had an innate talent for music that was obvious even when he was a toddler.

thai_meaning: สร้างสรรค์สิ่งใหม่ๆ, พัฒนานวัตกรรม
eng_definition: make changes in something established, especially by introducing new methods, ideas, or products
example_sentence: Companies must constantly innovate to stay competitive in the tech industry.

thai_meaning: การพูดเหน็บแนม, การบอกเป็นนัย(มักเป็นเรื่องเสื่อมเสีย)
eng_definition: an allusive or oblique remark or hint, typically a suggestive or disparaging one
example_sentence: The politician's speech was full of malicious innuendo about his opponent's private life.

thai_meaning: มากเกินไป, เกินพอดี
eng_definition: unusually or disproportionately large; excessive
example_sentence: He spent an inordinate amount of time playing video games instead of studying.

thai_meaning: การไต่สวน, การสอบถามข้อมูล
eng_definition: an act of asking for information; an official investigation
example_sentence: The government launched an official inquiry into the causes of the bridge collapse.

thai_meaning: ที่ไม่รู้จักพอ, กระหายอยากตลอดเวลา
eng_definition: (of an appetite or desire) impossible to satisfy
example_sentence: The young billionaire had an insatiable appetite for power and influence.

thai_meaning: ที่เป็นอันตรายแบบค่อยเป็นค่อยไป(โดยไม่รู้ตัว)
eng_definition: proceeding in a gradual, subtle way, but with harmful effects
example_sentence: High blood pressure is an insidious disease because it often has no visible symptoms.

thai_meaning: พูดเป็นนัยๆ (ในทางไม่ดี)
eng_definition: suggest or hint (something bad or reprehensible) in an indirect and unpleasant way
example_sentence: Are you trying to insinuate that I stole the money?

thai_meaning: อวดดี, ไร้มารยาท, ไม่เคารพผู้ใหญ่
eng_definition: showing a rude and arrogant lack of respect
example_sentence: The student was sent to the principal's office for his insolent behavior toward the teacher.

thai_meaning: ยุยงส่งเสริม(ให้เกิดเรื่อง), ริเริ่มดำเนินการ
eng_definition: bring about or initiate (an action or event); incite someone to do something, especially something bad
example_sentence: The rebellious group used social media to instigate a nationwide protest.

thai_meaning: ผู้ก่อการกบฏ, ผู้แข็งข้อ
eng_definition: a rebel or revolutionary
example_sentence: Heavily armed insurgents attempted to storm the capital building.

thai_meaning: ที่ไม่สามารถเอาชนะหรือก้าวข้ามไปได้
eng_definition: too great to be overcome
example_sentence: The debt they accumulated became an insurmountable barrier to keeping their business open.

thai_meaning: จับต้องไม่ได้, เป็นนามธรรม
eng_definition: unable to be touched or grasped; not having physical presence
example_sentence: Customer loyalty is an intangible asset that is difficult to measure in financial terms.

thai_meaning: ซึ่งเป็นส่วนสำคัญที่ขาดไม่ได้
eng_definition: necessary to make a whole complete; essential or fundamental
example_sentence: Regular exercise is an integral part of a healthy lifestyle.

thai_meaning: บูรณาการ, รวมเข้าด้วยกันเป็นหนึ่ง
eng_definition: combine (one thing) with another so that they become a whole
example_sentence: The new curriculum aims to integrate technology directly into the classroom experience.

thai_meaning: ความซื่อสัตย์สุจริต, ความสมบูรณ์ครบถ้วน
eng_definition: the quality of being honest and having strong moral principles; the state of being whole and undivided
example_sentence: She is known throughout the industry as a journalist of the highest integrity.

thai_meaning: ที่เกิดขึ้นเป็นช่วงๆ, ไม่ต่อเนื่อง
eng_definition: occurring at irregular intervals; not continuous or steady
example_sentence: The region experienced ___ rain showers throughout the afternoon.

thai_meaning: แทรกแซง, เข้ามาเกี่ยวข้องเพื่อแก้ไขสถานการณ์
eng_definition: come between so as to prevent or alter a result or course of events
example_sentence: The government had to ___ to prevent a total collapse of the banking system.

thai_meaning: ข่มขู่, ทำให้กลัว
eng_definition: frighten or overawe (someone), especially in order to make them do what one wants
example_sentence: The large dog was enough to ___ anyone who tried to enter the yard.

thai_meaning: การพิจารณาจิตใจตัวเอง, การทบทวนตัวเอง
eng_definition: the examination or observation of one's own mental and emotional processes
example_sentence: After the failure, he spent a lot of time in quiet ___.

thai_meaning: ท่วมท้น, ทะลักเข้ามาจำนวนมาก
eng_definition: overwhelm (someone) with things or people to be dealt with; flood
example_sentence: We were ___ with calls from angry customers after the website crashed.

thai_meaning: สินค้าคงคลัง, รายการสิ่งของ
eng_definition: a complete list of items such as property, goods in stock, or the contents of a building
example_sentence: The store stays closed for one day each year to take a complete ___ of its stock.

thai_meaning: ทำให้มีพลัง, ทำให้สดชื่น
eng_definition: give strength or energy to
example_sentence: A brisk walk in the fresh morning air will ___ you.

thai_meaning: ที่ไม่มีวันพ่ายแพ้, แข็งแกร่งเกินกว่าจะชนะได้
eng_definition: too powerful to be defeated or overcome
example_sentence: The team felt ___ after winning ten matches in a row.

thai_meaning: ที่ไม่อาจละเมิดได้, ศักดิ์สิทธิ์
eng_definition: never to be broken, infringed, or dishonored
example_sentence: The right to free speech is considered an ___ principle in many democracies.

thai_meaning: อ้างถึง(กฎหมาย), เรียกใช้(พลังหรือสิทธิ)
eng_definition: cite or appeal to (someone or something) as an authority for an action or in support of an argument
example_sentence: The police had to ___ a little-known law to make the arrest.

thai_meaning: ไร้ที่ติ, ไม่มีที่ให้ตำหนิ
eng_definition: beyond criticism; faultless
example_sentence: Her conduct as the company's CEO has been entirely ___.

thai_meaning: แผนการเดินทาง
eng_definition: a planned route or journey
example_sentence: We must follow the strict ___ if we want to visit all the museums today.

thai_meaning: เหนื่อยหน่าย, เบื่อโลก (เพราะเจอมาเยอะ)
eng_definition: tired, bored, or lacking enthusiasm, typically after having had too much of something
example_sentence: After years of traveling, he had become a bit ___ about luxury hotels.

thai_meaning: ภาษาเฉพาะกลุ่ม, ศัพท์เฉพาะทาง
eng_definition: special words or expressions used by a particular profession or group
example_sentence: The computer manual was filled with technical ___ that I couldn't understand.

thai_meaning: ร่าเริงแจ่มใส, มั่นใจในตัวเอง
eng_definition: having or expressing a lively, cheerful, and self-confident manner
example_sentence: He walked into the room with a ___ step and a bright smile.

thai_meaning: อันตราย, ความเสี่ยง
eng_definition: danger of loss, harm, or failure
example_sentence: The driver's reckless behavior put the lives of all the passengers in ___.

thai_meaning: สลัดทิ้ง, โยนของทิ้ง (มักใช้กับการทิ้งของจากเรือหรือเครื่องบิน)
eng_definition: throw or drop (something) from an aircraft or ship; abandon or discard
example_sentence: The pilot had to ___ some fuel to lighten the plane before the emergency landing.

thai_meaning: รอบคอบ, มีวิจารณญาณที่ดี
eng_definition: having, showing, or done with good judgment or sense
example_sentence: Success requires a ___ blend of hard work and careful planning.

thai_meaning: จุดเชื่อมต่อ, ทางแยก
eng_definition: a point where two or more things are joined
example_sentence: Turn left when you reach the main road ___.

thai_meaning: เขตอำนาจศาล, ขอบเขตของอำนาจหน้าที่
eng_definition: the official power to make legal decisions and judgments
example_sentence: The matter falls under the ___ of the local police, not the federal government.

thai_meaning: ให้เหตุผลสนับสนุน, พิสูจน์ว่าถูกต้อง
eng_definition: show or prove to be right or reasonable
example_sentence: It is difficult to ___ the high cost of the new building project.

thai_meaning: จุดไฟ, จุดประกาย(อารมณ์หรือความคิด)
eng_definition: light or set on fire; arouse or inspire (an emotion or feeling)
example_sentence: The teacher's words helped to ___ a newfound interest in science in the students.

thai_meaning: ที่เกี่ยวกับการเคลื่อนไหว, พลังงานจลน์
eng_definition: relating to or resulting from motion
example_sentence: The sculpture was composed of several ___ parts that moved in the wind.

thai_meaning: ความสามารถพิเศษ, ความชำนาญเฉพาะทาง
eng_definition: an acquired or natural skill at performing a task
example_sentence: He has a real ___ for remembering people's names after meeting them just once.

thai_meaning: คำชมเชย, ชื่อเสียง
eng_definition: praise and honor received for an achievement
example_sentence: She received a lot of ___ for her work on the community garden project.

thai_meaning: เปลี่ยนแปลงได้ง่าย (อารมณ์), ไม่คงที่
eng_definition: liable to change; easily altered; emotionally unstable
example_sentence: The patient's ___ emotional state made it difficult to provide consistent care.

thai_meaning: เขาวงกต, สิ่งที่ซับซ้อนยุ่งเหยิง
eng_definition: a complicated irregular network of passages or paths; a complex maze
example_sentence: Finding your way through the airport can be like navigating a ___.

thai_meaning: เฉื่อยชา, ไร้เรี่ยวแรง, อ่อนระโหย
eng_definition: (of a person, manner, or gesture) displaying or having a disinclination for physical exertion or effort
example_sentence: The warm weather made us all feel very ___ and sleepy.

thai_meaning: น่ายกย่อง, น่าสรรเสริญ
eng_definition: (of an action, idea, or goal) deserving praise and commendation
example_sentence: While his goal was ___ , the methods he used were highly questionable.

thai_meaning: หละหลวม, ไม่เข้มงวด
eng_definition: not sufficiently strict, severe, or careful
example_sentence: The company was criticized for its ___ security measures at the warehouse.

thai_meaning: ถูกต้องตามกฎหมาย, ชอบธรรม
eng_definition: conforming to the law or to rules
example_sentence: The government is considered the ___ authority in the country.

thai_meaning: การประสานงาน, ผู้ติดต่อประสานงาน
eng_definition: communication or cooperation which facilitates a close working relationship between people or organizations
example_sentence: He acted as a ___ between the police and the local community.

thai_meaning: เสรีนิยม, ใจกว้าง, มากมาย
eng_definition: willing to respect or accept behavior or opinions different from one's own; generous
example_sentence: The city is known for its ___ attitudes and diverse population.

thai_meaning: จุดสนใจ, การเป็นเป้าสายตาของสาธารณชน
eng_definition: the focus of public attention
example_sentence: The young actor was thrust into the ___ after his first major film role.

thai_meaning: เกี่ยวกับภาษา, ทางภาษาศาสตร์
eng_definition: relating to language or linguistics
example_sentence: Students in the program study both ___ theory and practical translation skills.

thai_meaning: ใจลอย, ไร้ความสนใจ, เซื่องซึม
eng_definition: (of a person or their manner) lacking energy or enthusiasm
example_sentence: The illness left him feeling ___ and unable to concentrate on his work.

thai_meaning: การร่ายยาว, รายการที่ซ้ำซากจำเจ
eng_definition: a series of petitions used in church services; a tedious recital or repetitive series
example_sentence: The customer provided a long ___ of complaints about the poor service.

thai_meaning: การฟ้องร้องดำเนินคดี
eng_definition: the process of taking legal action
example_sentence: The company is currently involved in several major ___ over patent disputes.

thai_meaning: เกลียดชังอย่างมาก
eng_definition: feel intense dislike or disgust for
example_sentence: They ___ the idea of having to work through the entire weekend.

thai_meaning: ห้องโถง, วิ่งเต้นโน้มน้าวใจ (นักการเมือง)
eng_definition: a hall or lobby in a building; seek to influence (a politician or public official) on an issue
example_sentence: Environmental groups continue to ___ the government for stricter emission laws.

thai_meaning: สูงส่ง, ทะเยอทะยาน
eng_definition: of imposing height; of a noble or elevated nature
example_sentence: He has ___ ambitions of becoming the next prime minister.

thai_meaning: เกี่ยวกับระบบการจัดการและการขนส่ง
eng_definition: relating to the detailed coordination of a complex operation involving many people, facilities, or supplies
example_sentence: Providing food and medicine to the disaster zone was a major ___ challenge.

thai_meaning: การมีอายุยืนยาว
eng_definition: long life; long existence or service
example_sentence: Good genes and a healthy diet are the keys to ___.

thai_meaning: ชัดเจน, เข้าใจง่าย, มีสติสัมปชัญญะ
eng_definition: expressed clearly; easy to understand; showing ability to think clearly
example_sentence: Even in her old age, she remained ___ and could remember events from her childhood.

thai_meaning: ที่ทำกำไรมหาศาล
eng_definition: producing a great deal of profit
example_sentence: He left his teaching job for a more ___ position in the private sector.

thai_meaning: น่าหัวเราะเยาะ, ไร้สาระสุดๆ
eng_definition: so foolish, unreasonable, or out of place as to be amusing; ridiculous
example_sentence: The idea that he could finish the entire project in one day is ___.

thai_meaning: สว่างไสว, สดใส
eng_definition: full of or shedding light; bright or shining, especially in the dark
example_sentence: The watch had ___ hands that glowed in the dark.

thai_meaning: ล่อใจ, ดึงดูด
eng_definition: tempt (a person or an animal) to do something or to go somewhere, especially by offering some form of reward
example_sentence: The promise of high salaries began to ___ many young doctors to the city.

thai_meaning: เป็นเงางาม, แวววาว
eng_definition: having luster; shining
example_sentence: She was famous for her long, ___ black hair.

thai_meaning: อุดมสมบูรณ์มาก, หรูหราเฟื่องฟู
eng_definition: (of vegetation) rich and profuse in growth; lush
example_sentence: The island is famous for its ___ tropical rainforests.

thai_meaning: หัวใจสำคัญ, บุคคลหรือสิ่งที่ขาดไม่ได้
eng_definition: a person or thing vital to an enterprise or organization
example_sentence: He was the ___ of the entire project; without him, it would have failed.

thai_meaning: เจ้าเล่ห์เพทุบาย (เพื่ออำนาจ)
eng_definition: cunning, scheming, and unscrupulous, especially in politics
example_sentence: He used ___ tactics to eliminate his rivals and become the CEO.

thai_meaning: ความใจกว้าง, การให้อภัย
eng_definition: the fact or condition of being magnanimous; generosity
example_sentence: In a display of great ___ , the winner praised his opponent's skill.

thai_meaning: ขนาดใหญ่, ความสำคัญมาก
eng_definition: the great size or extent of something
example_sentence: We did not realize the full ___ of the problem until it was too late.

thai_meaning: ความประสงค์ร้าย, ความพยาบาท
eng_definition: the intention or desire to do evil; ill will
example_sentence: She denied that there was any ___ intended in her comments.

thai_meaning: ใส่ร้าย, ให้ร้าย, เป็นอันตราย
eng_definition: evil in nature or effect; malevolent; speak about (someone) in a spitefully critical manner
example_sentence: It is unfair to ___ his character based on a single mistake.

thai_meaning: แสดงให้เห็นชัดเจน, ปรากฏชัด
eng_definition: clear or obvious to the eye or mind
example_sentence: Their dissatisfaction became ___ when they all refused to work.

thai_meaning: ควบคุมบังคับ, จัดการ(อย่างมีเล่ห์เหลี่ยม)
eng_definition: handle or control (a tool, mechanism, etc.), typically in a skillful manner; control or influence (a person or situation) cleverly or unscrupulously
example_sentence: She was accused of trying to ___ the stock market for her own profit.

thai_meaning: เล็กน้อยมาก, อยู่ที่ขอบ, ไม่ค่อยสำคัญ
eng_definition: relating to or at the edge or margin; minor and not important
example_sentence: The new policy had only a ___ effect on the company's overall profits.

thai_meaning: เกี่ยวกับทะเลหรือการเดินเรือ
eng_definition: connected with the sea, especially in relation to seafaring commercial or military activity
example_sentence: The country has a long and rich ___ history.

thai_meaning: ใหญ่โตมาก, มหึมา
eng_definition: large and heavy or solid; exceptionally large
example_sentence: A ___ earthquake caused widespread destruction across the region.

thai_meaning: เกี่ยวกับแม่, ที่มีความเป็นแม่
eng_definition: relating to a mother, especially during pregnancy or shortly after childbirth
example_sentence: She felt a strong ___ instinct to protect the small child.

thai_meaning: แม่พิมพ์, โครงสร้างหลัก, ตาราง
